message: Local P4a support slice implemented: platform operators can enter an audited writable single-tenant management context and platform tenant/ops lists can scope by all/group/org using group identity projected by platform SECURITY DEFINER functions.
evidence: Worktree local, not merged/released. Code: PlatformFeature::TenantManage; /api/platform/tenant-context start/exit; web MANAGE banner + 조직 관리 action; 0061 platform group scope identity migration; PlatformOrg/ops group fields; platform scope selector. Verification: cargo fmt --manifest-path backend/Cargo.toml --all; npm --workspace web run test -- UsersPage.test.tsx PlatformConsole.test.tsx ViewAsBanner.test.tsx auth.test.tsx --run (34 passed); npm run check:ts; npm run web:lint; npm run web:build; npm run check:openapi-app; sqlx migrate smoke through 0061; grouped-org smoke returned list/get/health scope-org:geurupsa:그룹사. GitHub refresh: open PRs 0; #20 latest P3/P4+ comments ingested; #24 still blocks live production freshness. Remaining G002: tenant-tier group-admin REST authz/grants, full org-tree/personnel/site-responsibility UI, approval/workflow graph, security sign-off.
User clarification 2026-06-26: essentially this is PBAC, ABAC, RBAC; these should be customizable within the UI/UX by managers with necessary permissions.
message: Current role lists and route guards are not sufficient for enterprise tenant/group/org policy management; policy customization needs its own durable tracked goal.

after.title: Policy studio — PBAC + ABAC + RBAC
after.objective: Add a manager/admin-customizable access policy studio that composes role grants (RBAC), attribute conditions (ABAC: group, tenant, org, department, team, position, employment status, assignment, location, device/security posture), and permission/purpose policies (PBAC: action/resource/purpose/sensitive-action/passkey-step-up). The UI must support safe templates, scope preview, diff/impact review, passkey step-up for publishing, audit logging, rollback, and guardrails so managers can only manage policies inside their delegated authority.
after.evidence: User clarification 2026-06-26: essentially this is PBAC, ABAC, RBAC; these should be customizable within the UI/UX by managers with necessary permissions.

message: Attempt 1

evidence: G002 complete: docs/specs/org-hierarchy.md updated; AccessScope enforcement centralizes live DB branch-scope resolution and live-scope intersection in mnt-platform-request-context + mnt-platform-authz; ordinary tenant REST/WS routes now call shared resolver and no production path trusts claims.branches; AccessScope::Group rejected outside RLS-preserving group fan-out; JWT group_roles canonicalized to GROUP_ADMIN/GROUP_VIEWER/GROUP_FINANCE on issue+verify. Verification: git diff --check backend/docs passed; cd backend && cargo fmt --check --all passed; cargo metadata --no-deps passed; static stale-principal scan passed (none missing shared resolver, no production claims.branches); static invariant checks passed; rust-analyzer stdin parse passed for changed Rust files. Independent review: code-reviewer APPROVE no blockers; architect CLEAR. Codex usage for story goal: 925605 tokens, 35m52s.

goals.title: Policy studio — PBAC + ABAC + RBAC
goals.objective: Add a manager/admin-customizable access policy studio that composes role grants (RBAC), attribute conditions (ABAC: group, tenant, org, department, team, position, employment status, assignment, location, device/security posture), and permission/purpose policies (PBAC: action/resource/purpose/sensitive-action/passkey-step-up). The UI must support safe templates, scope preview, diff/impact review, passkey step-up for publishing, audit logging, rollback, and guardrails so managers can only manage policies inside their delegated authority.
goals.evidence: User clarification 2026-06-26: essentially this is PBAC, ABAC, RBAC; these should be customizable within the UI/UX by managers with necessary permissions.

goals.title: Collaboration suite — messenger, mail, calendar, polls
goals.objective: Polish and wire an enterprise collaboration suite: messenger with support escalation/action cards/SLA context; mail inbox/thread workflow beyond settings; calendar with personal work calendar plus shared team/department/org/group calendars, availability, work-order/leave/approval overlays, and conflict visibility; polls/surveys with role-scoped audience targeting, anonymity controls, deadlines, reminders, results, and audit/compliance boundaries. UI must be role-aware, accessible, tested, and safe when backend support is staged.
goals.evidence: User requirement 2026-06-26: polish messenger, mail, calendar, and poll system; calendar has to be both shared(team/org/department etc) + personal(work) calendar.

goals.title: Native mobile collaboration + approvals
goals.objective: Polish the iOS Swift and Android Kotlin employee phone apps around today work/clock-in, passkey signing and sensitive-action authorization, notifications and alerts, approvals such as overtime, messenger, company mail, personal/shared calendar, and polls. Reuse generated APIs and reputable MIT/Apache dependencies only where commodity UI/protocol work should be adopted; own product flows, policy checks, auditability, tests, and native integration.
goals.evidence: User 2026-06-26: not just Swift but Kotlin for Android; phone app primary for clock-in, passkey auth/signing/authorization, notifications/alerts, approvals, messenger, mail, calendar, polls; end product must be well integrated and interact as if native.

goals.evidence: Active aggregate /goal in .omc/ultragoal/plans/conglomerate-platform/goals.json + ledger.jsonl covers G001-G015; marking story G001-track-a-stabilize-live-ops COMPLETE (G002-G015 remain active). IMPLEMENTATION: #19 core flows fixed+deployed (#19.13 mgmt_no normalize, #19.17 daily-plan visibility, #19.18 purchase create WORM-defer + error surfacing, #19.21 inquiry org-scope/storefront_org, #19.22 PM-schedule); codex P0 authz (OrgWideQueueTriage replaces is_admin_like widen; daily-plan gate; mgmt_no exact-then-unique binding+preview). Commits 3a87533..0755965. VALIDATION/REVIEW: review-gate (correctness+RLS-as-mnt_rt+codex cross-model+web) = GO-with-fixes, 2 mediums landed; security-reviewer on RBAC sub-spec (separate goal); real mnt_rt tests; backend gates PASSED; full workspace cargo test green after org_setup pagination fix. DEPLOYED LIVE: blue-green /readyz-smoke promotion to mnt-app@804d601+mnt-web@a28c85d, rollouts Healthy, live triager diagnostic done (1/9 admins low-risk). Blocked-external: #19.14 FLMS (Windows MSI). Orthogonal pre-existing CI debt (api-clients/E2E/Android) = task #47.

IMPLEMENTATION: #19 core flows fixed+deployed (#19.13 mgmt_no normalize, #19.17 daily-plan visibility, #19.18 purchase create WORM-defer + error surfacing, #19.21 inquiry org-scope/storefront_org, #19.22 PM-schedule); codex P0 authz (OrgWideQueueTriage replaces is_admin_like widen; daily-plan gate; mgmt_no exact-then-unique binding+preview). goals.title: Track B0 — Org-hierarchy security
goals.objective: Write org-hierarchy.md sub-spec + architect + security-review; AccessScope (Group→법인→branch→worksite) generalizing BranchScope + RLS-preserving consolidated reads
goals.evidence: G002 complete: docs/specs/org-hierarchy.md updated; AccessScope enforcement centralizes live DB branch-scope resolution and live-scope intersection in mnt-platform-request-context + mnt-platform-authz; ordinary tenant REST/WS routes now call shared resolver and no production path trusts claims.branches; AccessScope::Group rejected outside RLS-preserving group fan-out; JWT group_roles canonicalized to GROUP_ADMIN/GROUP_VIEWER/GROUP_FINANCE on issue+verify. Verification: git diff --check backend/docs passed; cd backend && cargo fmt --check --all passed; cargo metadata --no-deps passed; static stale-principal scan passed (none missing shared resolver, no production claims.branches); static invariant checks passed; rust-analyzer stdin parse passed for changed Rust files. goals.title: Policy studio — PBAC + ABAC + RBAC
goals.objective: Add a manager/admin-customizable access policy studio that composes role grants (RBAC), attribute conditions (ABAC: group, tenant, org, department, team, position, employment status, assignment, location, device/security posture), and permission/purpose policies (PBAC: action/resource/purpose/sensitive-action/passkey-step-up). The UI must support safe templates, scope preview, diff/impact review, passkey step-up for publishing, audit logging, rollback, and guardrails so managers can only manage policies inside their delegated authority.
goals.evidence: User clarification 2026-06-26: essentially this is PBAC, ABAC, RBAC; these should be customizable within the UI/UX by managers with necessary permissions.

goals.title: Collaboration suite — messenger, mail, calendar, polls
goals.objective: Polish and wire an enterprise collaboration suite: messenger with support escalation/action cards/SLA context; mail inbox/thread workflow beyond settings; calendar with personal work calendar plus shared team/department/org/group calendars, availability, work-order/leave/approval overlays, and conflict visibility; polls/surveys with role-scoped audience targeting, anonymity controls, deadlines, reminders, results, and audit/compliance boundaries. UI must be role-aware, accessible, tested, and safe when backend support is staged.
goals.evidence: User requirement 2026-06-26: polish messenger, mail, calendar, and poll system; calendar has to be both shared(team/org/department etc) + personal(work) calendar.

goals.title: Native mobile collaboration + approvals
goals.objective: Polish the iOS Swift and Android Kotlin employee phone apps around today work/clock-in, passkey signing and sensitive-action authorization, notifications and alerts, approvals such as overtime, messenger, company mail, personal/shared calendar, and polls. Reuse generated APIs and reputable MIT/Apache dependencies only where commodity UI/protocol work should be adopted; own product flows, policy checks, auditability, tests, and native integration.
goals.evidence: User 2026-06-26: not just Swift but Kotlin for Android; phone app primary for clock-in, passkey auth/signing/authorization, notifications/alerts, approvals, messenger, mail, calendar, polls; end product must be well integrated and interact as if native.

after.rationale: Prevents current and pending UI/API work from baking in a closed maintenance-role enum and makes custom role management a required platform capability.

evidence: G005 complete: added branch-scoped GET /api/v1/equipment/{id} for imported equipment object links, with EquipmentReadQuery, PgRegistryStore::get_equipment under with_org_conn/RLS, REST get_equipment read authorization, OpenAPI getEquipment, and generated TS/Kotlin/Swift clients. Dynamic/customizable PBAC/ABAC/RBAC roles remain explicitly tracked as G016; this slice did not widen static role architecture beyond existing UI edit affordance gates. Regression: added backend SQLx GET-by-id/404 test (not run because direct cargo test/check is forbidden by repo hook) and web detail/browse tests. Verification: npm --workspace web run test -- src/pages/EquipmentDetailPage.test.tsx src/pages/EquipmentBrowsePage.test.tsx src/features/equipment/EquipmentManagementPanel.test.tsx src/features/equipment/equipment-format.test.ts src/features/object-view/ObjectViewScaffold.test.tsx src/EmptyBackend.test.tsx = 6 files/25 tests passed; npm run check:ts passed; npm --workspace web run lint passed; npm --workspace web run build passed; cd backend && cargo fmt --all && cargo fmt --all -- --check passed; cargo metadata --format-version 1 --no-deps passed with 75 packages/75 workspace members; TS/Kotlin/Swift client regeneration was deterministic/no-op after generation; npm run check:kotlin BUILD SUCCESSFUL; npm run check:swift Build complete; git diff --check passed. Independent review: code-reviewer APPROVE no issues, confirmed no 2000-row list scan remains and branch/RLS boundaries preserved; architect CLEAR, confirmed minimal future-compatible contract and policy-studio gap bounded to G016. Codex story goal complete: 605046 tokens, 26m59s.

goals.title: Policy studio — PBAC + ABAC + RBAC
goals.objective: Add a manager/admin-customizable access policy studio that composes role grants (RBAC), attribute conditions (ABAC: group, tenant, org, department, team, position, employment status, assignment, location, device/security posture), and permission/purpose policies (PBAC: action/resource/purpose/sensitive-action/passkey-step-up). The UI must support safe templates, scope preview, diff/impact review, passkey step-up for publishing, audit logging, rollback, and guardrails so managers can only manage policies inside their delegated authority.
goals.evidence: User clarification 2026-06-26: essentially this is PBAC, ABAC, RBAC; these should be customizable within the UI/UX by managers with necessary permissions.

message: Advance G016 incrementally: users need many customizable roles, but safe enterprise policy management must stage definitions and planned assignments before effective auth publishing. This slice adds assignable custom-role planning data without widening users.roles or runtime authorization, while keeping passkey publish, impact diff, rollback, ABAC/PBAC conditions, and effective resolver as remaining G016 work.

after.evidence: G016-P1a planned custom-role assignments implemented: REST GET /api/v1/policy/assignments and PUT /api/v1/policy/users/{id}/assignments, PgOrgStore list/replace assignment methods under org-armed RLS, custom-role-only validation, target-user branch-scope precheck, audit events with before/after snapshots, policy_version bump, OpenAPI/client regeneration, and Policy Studio user/role assignment planner with explicit warning that assignments are not runtime-effective auth. Code-reviewer found two medium OpenAPI contract mismatches; fixed by requiring CreatePolicyRoleRequest.permissions with minItems=1 and documenting 404 for listPolicyAssignments, then regenerated TS/Kotlin/Swift. Verification green: git diff --check; backend cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo metadata --format-version 1 --no-deps packages=75 workspace_members=75; npm run check:ts; npm --workspace web run lint; npm --workspace web run test -- src/pages/PolicyStudioPage.test.tsx src/components/shell/nav.test.ts (2 files, 25 tests); npm --workspace web run build; npm run check:kotlin BUILD SUCCESSFUL; npm run check:swift Build complete. Backend full OpenAPI/app compile gate remains an explicit environment/time gap: npm run check:openapi-app timed out after 240s during backend compilation, and direct focused cargo checks previously timed out in sqlx before reaching changed crates; no lingering cargo/rustc processes.
after.rationale: Advance G016 incrementally: users need many customizable roles, but safe enterprise policy management must stage definitions and planned assignments before effective auth publishing. This slice adds assignable custom-role planning data without widening users.roles or runtime authorization, while keeping passkey publish, impact diff, rollback, ABAC/PBAC conditions, and effective resolver as remaining G016 work.

goals.title: Policy studio — PBAC + ABAC + RBAC
goals.objective: Add a manager/admin-customizable access policy studio that composes role grants (RBAC), attribute conditions (ABAC: group, tenant, org, department, team, position, employment status, assignment, location, device/security posture), and permission/purpose policies (PBAC: action/resource/purpose/sensitive-action/passkey-step-up). The UI must support safe templates, scope preview, diff/impact review, passkey step-up for publishing, audit logging, rollback, and guardrails so managers can only manage policies inside their delegated authority.
goals.evidence: User clarification 2026-06-26: essentially this is PBAC, ABAC, RBAC; these should be customizable within the UI/UX by managers with necessary permissions.

goals.title: Collaboration suite — messenger, mail, calendar, polls
goals.objective: Polish and wire an enterprise collaboration suite: messenger with support escalation/action cards/SLA context; mail inbox/thread workflow beyond settings; calendar with personal work calendar plus shared team/department/org/group calendars, availability, work-order/leave/approval overlays, and conflict visibility; polls/surveys with role-scoped audience targeting, anonymity controls, deadlines, reminders, results, and audit/compliance boundaries. UI must be role-aware, accessible, tested, and safe when backend support is staged.
goals.evidence: User requirement 2026-06-26: polish messenger, mail, calendar, and poll system; calendar has to be both shared(team/org/department etc) + personal(work) calendar.

goals.title: Native mobile collaboration + approvals
goals.objective: Polish the iOS Swift and Android Kotlin employee phone apps around today work/clock-in, passkey signing and sensitive-action authorization, notifications and alerts, approvals such as overtime, messenger, company mail, personal/shared calendar, and polls. Reuse generated APIs and reputable MIT/Apache dependencies only where commodity UI/protocol work should be adopted; own product flows, policy checks, auditability, tests, and native integration.
goals.evidence: User 2026-06-26: not just Swift but Kotlin for Android; phone app primary for clock-in, passkey auth/signing/authorization, notifications/alerts, approvals, messenger, mail, calendar, polls; end product must be well integrated and interact as if native.

goals.title: Track A — Stabilize live ops
goals.objective: Fix issue-#19 broken-loop flows (#40 batches) + remaining #19; every flow create→admin-visible round-trip tested as real mnt_rt
goals.evidence: Active aggregate /goal in .omc/ultragoal/plans/conglomerate-platform/goals.json + ledger.jsonl covers G001-G015; marking story G001-track-a-stabilize-live-ops COMPLETE (G002-G015 remain active). IMPLEMENTATION: #19 core flows fixed+deployed (#19.13 mgmt_no normalize, #19.17 daily-plan visibility, #19.18 purchase create WORM-defer + error surfacing, #19.21 inquiry org-scope/storefront_org, #19.22 PM-schedule); codex P0 authz (OrgWideQueueTriage replaces is_admin_like widen; daily-plan gate; mgmt_no exact-then-unique binding+preview). Commits 3a87533..0755965. VALIDATION/REVIEW: review-gate (correctness+RLS-as-mnt_rt+codex cross-model+web) = GO-with-fixes, 2 mediums landed; security-reviewer on RBAC sub-spec (separate goal); real mnt_rt tests; backend gates PASSED; full workspace cargo test green after org_setup pagination fix. DEPLOYED LIVE: blue-green /readyz-smoke promotion to mnt-app@804d601+mnt-web@a28c85d, rollouts Healthy, live triager diagnostic done (1/9 admins low-risk). Blocked-external: #19.14 FLMS (Windows MSI). Orthogonal pre-existing CI debt (api-clients/E2E/Android) = task #47.

evidence: G016-P1b preview implemented and regression-locked: POST /api/v1/policy/users/{id}/assignment-preview computes current-vs-requested custom-role assignment delta and feature grants for visible users, is RoleManage-gated, branch/access-scope checked, rejects unknown/retired/system policy role IDs, returns effective=false and preview-only warnings, and performs no writes/no runtime auth mutation. Fresh verification green: git diff --check; backend cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo metadata packages=75 workspace_members=75; containerized SQLX_OFFLINE docker cargo test -p mnt-identity-rest policy_assignment_preview (2 passed); npm run check:ts -- --pretty false; npm --workspace web run test -- src/pages/PolicyStudioPage.test.tsx src/components/shell/nav.test.ts (25 passed); npm --workspace web run lint; npm --workspace web run build; npm run check:kotlin BUILD SUCCESSFUL; npm run check:swift Build complete; npm run check:openapi-app served /healthz and /openapi/openapi.yaml.
message: Adds required impact/scope preview before any future passkey publish/effective authorization. This advances dynamic role management safely without widening users.roles or runtime authorization, and locks the security-sensitive no-write contract before the next policy-system increment.

after.evidence: G016-P1b preview implemented and regression-locked: POST /api/v1/policy/users/{id}/assignment-preview computes current-vs-requested custom-role assignment delta and feature grants for visible users, is RoleManage-gated, branch/access-scope checked, rejects unknown/retired/system policy role IDs, returns effective=false and preview-only warnings, and performs no writes/no runtime auth mutation. UI Policy Studio includes impact preview panel before save; OpenAPI and TS/Kotlin/Swift clients regenerated. Reviewer LOW coverage finding closed with backend identity REST integration tests: non-RoleManage forbidden, branch-scoped superadmin cannot preview out-of-scope target, unknown/retired/system IDs rejected, valid preview returns delta/grants, and persisted assignments remain unchanged. Fresh verification green: git diff --check; backend cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo metadata packages=75 workspace_members=75; containerized SQLX_OFFLINE docker cargo test -p mnt-identity-rest policy_assignment_preview (2 passed); npm run check:ts -- --pretty false; npm --workspace web run test -- src/pages/PolicyStudioPage.test.tsx src/components/shell/nav.test.ts (25 passed); npm --workspace web run lint; npm --workspace web run build; npm run check:kotlin BUILD SUCCESSFUL; npm run check:swift Build complete; npm run check:openapi-app served /healthz and /openapi/openapi.yaml.
after.rationale: Adds required impact/scope preview before any future passkey publish/effective authorization. This advances dynamic role management safely without widening users.roles or runtime authorization, and locks the security-sensitive no-write contract before the next policy-system increment.

goals.title: Policy studio — PBAC + ABAC + RBAC
goals.objective: Add a manager/admin-customizable access policy studio that composes role grants (RBAC), attribute conditions (ABAC: group, tenant, org, department, team, position, employment status, assignment, location, device/security posture), and permission/purpose policies (PBAC: action/resource/purpose/sensitive-action/passkey-step-up). The UI must support safe templates, scope preview, diff/impact review, passkey step-up for publishing, audit logging, rollback, and guardrails so managers can only manage policies inside their delegated authority.
goals.evidence: User clarification 2026-06-26: essentially this is PBAC, ABAC, RBAC; these should be customizable within the UI/UX by managers with necessary permissions.

goals.title: Collaboration suite — messenger, mail, calendar, polls

goals.title: Native mobile collaboration + approvals
goals.objective: Polish the iOS Swift and Android Kotlin employee phone apps around today work/clock-in, passkey signing and sensitive-action authorization, notifications and alerts, approvals such as overtime, messenger, company mail, personal/shared calendar, and polls. Reuse generated APIs and reputable MIT/Apache dependencies only where commodity UI/protocol work should be adopted; own product flows, policy checks, auditability, tests, and native integration.
goals.evidence: User 2026-06-26: not just Swift but Kotlin for Android; phone app primary for clock-in, passkey auth/signing/authorization, notifications/alerts, approvals, messenger, mail, calendar, polls; end product must be well integrated and interact as if native.

evidence: G016-P1c role templates implemented: GET /api/v1/policy/role-templates returns RoleManage-gated built-in starter templates for common operations, finance, people, assets, and customer roles. Verification green: cd backend && cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo metadata --manifest-path backend/Cargo.toml --format-version 1 --no-deps = packages 75 workspace_members 75; npm run gen:api; npm run check:ts -- --pretty false; npm --workspace web run test -- src/pages/PolicyStudioPage.test.tsx src/components/shell/nav.test.ts = 2 files/26 tests passed; npm --workspace web run lint; npm --workspace web run build; npm run check:kotlin = BUILD SUCCESSFUL; npm run check:swift = Build complete; docker Rust 1.96 unit test mnt-identity-rest role_templates_are_unique_non_empty_and_never_grant_elevated_policy_features passed; docker Rust 1.96 mnt-app openapi_yaml_covers_mounted_auth_routes passed; git diff --check passed. Note: npm run check:openapi-app on host was attempted twice but host rustc stalled at 0 percent CPU inside cargo build; Docker Rust 1.96 route-coverage test was used as the fresh app/OpenAPI fallback.
message: Supports the user requirement to create many different roles safely: managers get editable starter roles for diverse business functions without widening effective authorization until passkey-gated publish, scope preview, rollback, and audit guardrails are implemented.

after.evidence: G016-P1c role templates implemented: GET /api/v1/policy/role-templates returns RoleManage-gated built-in starter templates for common operations, finance, people, assets, and customer roles. Templates are typed from canonical Feature/PermissionLevel values, never include elevated RoleManage/ElevatedRoleGrant, copy into the real custom-role create form, remain editable before save, and do not publish runtime authorization. OpenAPI regenerated TS/Kotlin/Swift clients expose listPolicyRoleTemplates and PolicyRoleTemplateResponse. Verification green: cd backend && cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo metadata --manifest-path backend/Cargo.toml --format-version 1 --no-deps = packages 75 workspace_members 75; npm run gen:api; npm run check:ts -- --pretty false; npm --workspace web run test -- src/pages/PolicyStudioPage.test.tsx src/components/shell/nav.test.ts = 2 files/26 tests passed; npm --workspace web run lint; npm --workspace web run build; npm run check:kotlin = BUILD SUCCESSFUL; npm run check:swift = Build complete; docker Rust 1.96 unit test mnt-identity-rest role_templates_are_unique_non_empty_and_never_grant_elevated_policy_features passed; docker Rust 1.96 mnt-app openapi_yaml_covers_mounted_auth_routes passed; git diff --check passed. Note: npm run check:openapi-app on host was attempted twice but host rustc stalled at 0 percent CPU inside cargo build; Docker Rust 1.96 route-coverage test was used as the fresh app/OpenAPI fallback.
after.rationale: Supports the user requirement to create many different roles safely: managers get editable starter roles for diverse business functions without widening effective authorization until passkey-gated publish, scope preview, rollback, and audit guardrails are implemented.

goals.title: Policy studio — PBAC + ABAC + RBAC
goals.objective: Add a manager/admin-customizable access policy studio that composes role grants (RBAC), attribute conditions (ABAC: group, tenant, org, department, team, position, employment status, assignment, location, device/security posture), and permission/purpose policies (PBAC: action/resource/purpose/sensitive-action/passkey-step-up). The UI must support safe templates, scope preview, diff/impact review, passkey step-up for publishing, audit logging, rollback, and guardrails so managers can only manage policies inside their delegated authority.
goals.evidence: User clarification 2026-06-26: essentially this is PBAC, ABAC, RBAC; these should be customizable within the UI/UX by managers with necessary permissions.

goals.title: Policy studio — PBAC + ABAC + RBAC
goals.objective: Add a manager/admin-customizable access policy studio that composes role grants (RBAC), attribute conditions (ABAC: group, tenant, org, department, team, position, employment status, assignment, location, device/security posture), and permission/purpose policies (PBAC: action/resource/purpose/sensitive-action/passkey-step-up). The UI must support safe templates, scope preview, diff/impact review, passkey step-up for publishing, audit logging, rollback, and guardrails so managers can only manage policies inside their delegated authority.
goals.evidence: User clarification 2026-06-26: essentially this is PBAC, ABAC, RBAC; these should be customizable within the UI/UX by managers with necessary permissions.

goals.title: Native mobile collaboration + approvals

Reuse generated APIs and reputable MIT/Apache dependencies only where commodity UI/protocol work should be adopted; own product flows, policy checks, auditability, tests, and native integration.
goals.evidence: User 2026-06-26: not just Swift but Kotlin for Android; phone app primary for clock-in, passkey auth/signing/authorization, notifications/alerts, approvals, messenger, mail, calendar, polls; end product must be well integrated and interact as if native.

goals.title: Policy studio — PBAC + ABAC + RBAC
goals.objective: Add a manager/admin-customizable access policy studio that composes role grants (RBAC), attribute conditions (ABAC: group, tenant, org, department, team, position, employment status, assignment, location, device/security posture), and permission/purpose policies (PBAC: action/resource/purpose/sensitive-action/passkey-step-up). The UI must support safe templates, scope preview, diff/impact review, passkey step-up for publishing, audit logging, rollback, and guardrails so managers can only manage policies inside their delegated authority.
goals.evidence: User clarification 2026-06-26: essentially this is PBAC, ABAC, RBAC; these should be customizable within the UI/UX by managers with necessary permissions.

goals.title: Collaboration suite — messenger, mail, calendar, polls
goals.objective: Polish and wire an enterprise collaboration suite: messenger with support escalation/action cards/SLA context; mail inbox/thread workflow beyond settings; calendar with personal work calendar plus shared team/department/org/group calendars, availability, work-order/leave/approval overlays, and conflict visibility; polls/surveys with role-scoped audience targeting, anonymity controls, deadlines, reminders, results, and audit/compliance boundaries. UI must be role-aware, accessible, tested, and safe when backend support is staged.
goals.evidence: User requirement 2026-06-26: polish messenger, mail, calendar, and poll system; calendar has to be both shared(team/org/department etc) + personal(work) calendar.

goals.title: Native mobile collaboration + approvals
goals.objective: Polish the iOS Swift and Android Kotlin employee phone apps around today work/clock-in, passkey signing and sensitive-action authorization, notifications and alerts, approvals such as overtime, messenger, company mail, personal/shared calendar, and polls. Reuse generated APIs and reputable MIT/Apache dependencies only where commodity UI/protocol work should be adopted; own product flows, policy checks, auditability, tests, and native integration.
goals.evidence: User 2026-06-26: not just Swift but Kotlin for Android; phone app primary for clock-in, passkey auth/signing/authorization, notifications/alerts, approvals, messenger, mail, calendar, polls; end product must be well integrated and interact as if native.

goals.evidence: G005 complete: added branch-scoped GET /api/v1/equipment/{id} for imported equipment object links, with EquipmentReadQuery, PgRegistryStore::get_equipment under with_org_conn/RLS, REST get_equipment read authorization, OpenAPI getEquipment, and generated TS/Kotlin/Swift clients. Web /equipment/:id now uses the shared ObjectViewScaffold and typed GET-by-id instead of scanning /equipment/list, and browse rows preserve quick-view while the action link navigates to /equipment/{id}. Dynamic/customizable PBAC/ABAC/RBAC roles remain explicitly tracked as G016; this slice did not widen static role architecture beyond existing UI edit affordance gates. Regression: added backend SQLx GET-by-id/404 test (not run because direct cargo test/check is forbidden by repo hook) and web detail/browse tests. Verification: npm --workspace web run test -- src/pages/EquipmentDetailPage.test.tsx src/pages/EquipmentBrowsePage.test.tsx src/features/equipment/EquipmentManagementPanel.test.tsx src/features/equipment/equipment-format.test.ts src/features/object-view/ObjectViewScaffold.test.tsx src/EmptyBackend.test.tsx = 6 files/25 tests passed; npm run check:ts passed; npm --workspace web run lint passed; npm --workspace web run build passed; cd backend && cargo fmt --all && cargo fmt --all -- --check passed; cargo metadata --format-version 1 --no-deps passed with 75 packages/75 workspace members; TS/Kotlin/Swift client regeneration was deterministic/no-op after generation; npm run check:kotlin BUILD SUCCESSFUL; npm run check:swift Build complete; git diff --check passed. Independent review: code-reviewer APPROVE no issues, confirmed no 2000-row list scan remains and branch/RLS boundaries preserved; architect CLEAR, confirmed minimal future-compatible contract and policy-studio gap bounded to G016. Codex story goal complete: 605046 tokens, 26m59s.

after.evidence: P1d complete: PATCH /api/v1/policy/roles/{id}/status added with RoleManage + fresh PasskeyService::verify_step_up_for_user gate; missing step_up returns 428 before mutation; PgOrgStore rejects system roles, updates custom role lifecycle, bumps policy version on change, and writes audit snapshot; Policy Studio custom-role actions 게시/초안으로 되돌리기/퇴역 use assertPasskeyStepUp before PATCH; TS/Kotlin/Swift clients regenerated including UpdatePolicyRoleStatusRequest. Verification: npm run gen:api; cd backend && cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo metadata packages 75 workspace_members 75; npm run check:ts -- --pretty false; npm --workspace web run test -- src/pages/PolicyStudioPage.test.tsx src/components/shell/nav.test.ts passed 2 files/27 tests; npm --workspace web run lint; npm --workspace web run build; npm run check:kotlin BUILD SUCCESSFUL; npm run check:swift Build complete; docker rust:1.96 identity REST policy_role_status_update passed 1 test; docker rust:1.96 mnt-app openapi_yaml_covers_mounted_auth_routes passed 1 test; git diff --check.
after.rationale: This closes the G016 custom-role lifecycle/publish gap with a real actor-bound passkey step-up instead of a shape-only placeholder, while preserving later runtime-effective resolver work as a separate slice.

evidence: P1e complete: custom policy roles now persist and return ABAC/PBAC condition metadata via tenant-scoped policy_role_conditions, REST DTOs, OpenAPI/generated TS/Kotlin/Swift clients, and Policy Studio condition editor/summaries. Fresh verification: npm run check:ts -- --pretty false passed; npm --workspace web run test -- src/pages/PolicyStudioPage.test.tsx passed 5/5; npm --workspace web run lint passed; npm --workspace web run build passed; npm run check:kotlin passed; npm run check:swift passed; backend cargo fmt --all -- --check passed; cargo metadata reported packages 75 workspace_members 75; git diff --check passed; Docker rust:1.96 targeted mnt-identity-rest tests passed policy_condition_validation and two policy_role_create_ integration tests; lightweight OpenAPI/generated readback passed. Optional full mnt-app openapi_yaml_covers_mounted_auth_routes was stopped after prolonged Docker link silence.
message: This advances the many-different-roles requirement by letting custom roles combine RBAC grants with scoped ABAC/PBAC metadata while keeping runtime policy-resolution, impact preview, and publish guardrails for the next guarded slices.

goals.title: Policy studio — PBAC + ABAC + RBAC
goals.objective: Add a manager/admin-customizable access policy studio that composes role grants (RBAC), attribute conditions (ABAC: group, tenant, org, department, team, position, employment status, assignment, location, device/security posture), and permission/purpose policies (PBAC: action/resource/purpose/sensitive-action/passkey-step-up). The UI must support safe templates, scope preview, diff/impact review, passkey step-up for publishing, audit logging, rollback, and guardrails so managers can only manage policies inside their delegated authority.
goals.evidence: User clarification 2026-06-26: essentially this is PBAC, ABAC, RBAC; these should be customizable within the UI/UX by managers with necessary permissions.

goals.title: Collaboration suite — messenger, mail, calendar, polls
goals.objective: Polish and wire an enterprise collaboration suite: messenger with support escalation/action cards/SLA context; mail inbox/thread workflow beyond settings; calendar with personal work calendar plus shared team/department/org/group calendars, availability, work-order/leave/approval overlays, and conflict visibility; polls/surveys with role-scoped audience targeting, anonymity controls, deadlines, reminders, results, and audit/compliance boundaries. UI must be role-aware, accessible, tested, and safe when backend support is staged.
goals.evidence: User requirement 2026-06-26: polish messenger, mail, calendar, and poll system; calendar has to be both shared(team/org/department etc) + personal(work) calendar.

goals.title: Native mobile collaboration + approvals
goals.objective: Polish the iOS Swift and Android Kotlin employee phone apps around today work/clock-in, passkey signing and sensitive-action authorization, notifications and alerts, approvals such as overtime, messenger, company mail, personal/shared calendar, and polls. Reuse generated APIs and reputable MIT/Apache dependencies only where commodity UI/protocol work should be adopted; own product flows, policy checks, auditability, tests, and native integration.
goals.evidence: User 2026-06-26: not just Swift but Kotlin for Android; phone app primary for clock-in, passkey auth/signing/authorization, notifications/alerts, approvals, messenger, mail, calendar, polls; end product must be well integrated and interact as if native.

goals.title: Track A — Stabilize live ops
goals.objective: Fix issue-#19 broken-loop flows (#40 batches) + remaining #19; every flow create→admin-visible round-trip tested as real mnt_rt
goals.evidence: Active aggregate /goal in .omc/ultragoal/plans/conglomerate-platform/goals.json + ledger.jsonl covers G001-G015; marking story G001-track-a-stabilize-live-ops COMPLETE (G002-G015 remain active). IMPLEMENTATION: #19 core flows fixed+deployed (#19.13 mgmt_no normalize, #19.17 daily-plan visibility, #19.18 purchase create WORM-defer + error surfacing, #19.21 inquiry org-scope/storefront_org, #19.22 PM-schedule); codex P0 authz (OrgWideQueueTriage replaces is_admin_like widen; daily-plan gate; mgmt_no exact-then-unique binding+preview). Commits 3a87533..0755965. VALIDATION/REVIEW: review-gate (correctness+RLS-as-mnt_rt+codex cross-model+web) = GO-with-fixes, 2 mediums landed; security-reviewer on RBAC sub-spec (separate goal); real mnt_rt tests; backend gates PASSED; full workspace cargo test green after org_setup pagination fix. DEPLOYED LIVE: blue-green /readyz-smoke promotion to mnt-app@804d601+mnt-web@a28c85d, rollouts Healthy, live triager diagnostic done (1/9 admins low-risk). Blocked-external: #19.14 FLMS (Windows MSI). Orthogonal pre-existing CI debt (api-clients/E2E/Android) = task #47.

goals.title: Policy studio — PBAC + ABAC + RBAC
goals.objective: Add a manager/admin-customizable access policy studio that composes role grants (RBAC), attribute conditions (ABAC: group, tenant, org, department, team, position, employment status, assignment, location, device/security posture), and permission/purpose policies (PBAC: action/resource/purpose/sensitive-action/passkey-step-up). The UI must support safe templates, scope preview, diff/impact review, passkey step-up for publishing, audit logging, rollback, and guardrails so managers can only manage policies inside their delegated authority.
goals.evidence: User clarification 2026-06-26: essentially this is PBAC, ABAC, RBAC; these should be customizable within the UI/UX by managers with necessary permissions.

goals.title: Collaboration suite — messenger, mail, calendar, polls
goals.objective: Polish and wire an enterprise collaboration suite: messenger with support escalation/action cards/SLA context; mail inbox/thread workflow beyond settings; calendar with personal work calendar plus shared team/department/org/group calendars, availability, work-order/leave/approval overlays, and conflict visibility; polls/surveys with role-scoped audience targeting, anonymity controls, deadlines, reminders, results, and audit/compliance boundaries. UI must be role-aware, accessible, tested, and safe when backend support is staged.

goals.title: Native mobile collaboration + approvals
goals.objective: Polish the iOS Swift and Android Kotlin employee phone apps around today work/clock-in, passkey signing and sensitive-action authorization, notifications and alerts, approvals such as overtime, messenger, company mail, personal/shared calendar, and polls. Reuse generated APIs and reputable MIT/Apache dependencies only where commodity UI/protocol work should be adopted; own product flows, policy checks, auditability, tests, and native integration.
goals.evidence: User 2026-06-26: not just Swift but Kotlin for Android; phone app primary for clock-in, passkey auth/signing/authorization, notifications/alerts, approvals, messenger, mail, calendar, polls; end product must be well integrated and interact as if native.

goals.title: Policy studio — PBAC + ABAC + RBAC
goals.objective: Add a manager/admin-customizable access policy studio that composes role grants (RBAC), attribute conditions (ABAC: group, tenant, org, department, team, position, employment status, assignment, location, device/security posture), and permission/purpose policies (PBAC: action/resource/purpose/sensitive-action/passkey-step-up). The UI must support safe templates, scope preview, diff/impact review, passkey step-up for publishing, audit logging, rollback, and guardrails so managers can only manage policies inside their delegated authority.
goals.evidence: User clarification 2026-06-26: essentially this is PBAC, ABAC, RBAC; these should be customizable within the UI/UX by managers with necessary permissions.

goals.title: Track D — Webmail enable
goals.objective: #28 Vault key + #39 inbound hardening + webmail client UI (DOMPurify gate) + unify inquiries (#42)

goals.title: Track D — Dispatch map
goals.objective: #29 clock-in-gated live location + arrival/return markers + routing

goals.title: Collaboration suite — messenger, mail, calendar, polls
goals.objective: Polish and wire an enterprise collaboration suite: messenger with support escalation/action cards/SLA context; mail inbox/thread workflow beyond settings; calendar with personal work calendar plus shared team/department/org/group calendars, availability, work-order/leave/approval overlays, and conflict visibility; polls/surveys with role-scoped audience targeting, anonymity controls, deadlines, reminders, results, and audit/compliance boundaries. UI must be role-aware, accessible, tested, and safe when backend support is staged.
goals.evidence: User requirement 2026-06-26: polish messenger, mail, calendar, and poll system; calendar has to be both shared(team/org/department etc) + personal(work) calendar.

goals.title: Native mobile collaboration + approvals
goals.objective: Polish the iOS Swift and Android Kotlin employee phone apps around today work/clock-in, passkey signing and sensitive-action authorization, notifications and alerts, approvals such as overtime, messenger, company mail, personal/shared calendar, and polls. Reuse generated APIs and reputable MIT/Apache dependencies only where commodity UI/protocol work should be adopted; own product flows, policy checks, auditability, tests, and native integration.
goals.evidence: User 2026-06-26: not just Swift but Kotlin for Android; phone app primary for clock-in, passkey auth/signing/authorization, notifications/alerts, approvals, messenger, mail, calendar, polls; end product must be well integrated and interact as if native.

goals.evidence: User clarification 2026-06-26: essentially this is PBAC, ABAC, RBAC; these should be customizable within the UI/UX by managers with necessary permissions.

goals.title: Native mobile collaboration + approvals

after.evidence: G016 P1g/observability benchmark increment complete: delegated branch-scoped RoleManage guardrails now prevent branch-scoped policy managers from creating/listing/previewing/assigning custom roles outside their branch condition scope; list hides out-of-scope custom roles; invalid branch condition UUIDs fail validation; out-of-scope branch conditions fail closed. Policy Studio observability was raised toward the Palantir/Foundry enterprise benchmark: docs/benchmarks/palantir-foundry-ops-benchmark.md added, wired from docs/ENTERPRISE-READINESS.md and docs/specs/rbac-configurable.md; identity/rest emits structured policy_studio_* logs with stable event names, actor UUIDs only (no email/PII), audit trace/span correlation for mutations, and low-cardinality policy_studio_operation_total{operation,outcome} metrics for list/create/update/assignment/preview success plus delegated-scope denials/invalids. app health_readiness metrics test now asserts policy_studio_operation_total bounded labels are scrapeable; full mnt-app test binary execution was attempted in Docker rust:1.96 but killed after a known app-link stall, so not counted as passed; Docker Rust 1.96 app test typecheck passed instead. Fresh verification passed: Rust workspace formatter strict check; Rust metadata reported packages 75 workspace_members 75; Docker rust:1.96 targeted mnt-identity-rest integration tests for branch-scoped policy managers, assignment preview mutation safety, and policy-role create condition handling all passed; npm run check:ts -- --pretty false passed; npm workspace web PolicyStudioPage test passed 5/5; npm workspace web lint passed; npm workspace web build passed; npm run check:kotlin passed; npm run check:swift passed; git diff --check passed.
after.rationale: This closes the immediate delegated-authority gap and makes observability a release gate instead of an afterthought, while honestly preserving remaining G016 work: live runtime policy resolver, publish/rollback, full Playwright/visual user-story evidence, and production Alertmanager/runbook test-fire still remain before claiming enterprise/Palantir-grade completion.

evidence: Changed backend identity application/adapter/rest to return PolicyVersionResponse from GET /api/v1/policy/roles; UI shows KST policy-version lineage and passkey-labelled lifecycle actions; OpenAPI TS/Kotlin/Swift regenerated including PolicyVersionResponse; docs/specs/rbac-configurable.md updated. Verification: backend rustfmt check passed; metadata summary packages=75 workspace_members=75; npm gen:api passed; TypeScript check passed; PolicyStudioPage vitest 5/5 passed; web lint passed; web build passed; Kotlin client build passed; Swift client build passed; Docker Rust 1.96 identity integration tests passed for policy version bump, ABAC/PBAC catalog, and branch delegated guardrails; git diff whitespace check passed. Gap: npm run check:openapi-app was stopped after host mnt-app build hung in zero-CPU rustc for identity adapter for about five minutes; no code error output was produced, and route/schema impact is otherwise covered by generated clients plus targeted backend tests.
message: P1h policy version lineage: surfaced tenant policy_version through Policy Studio role catalog API, generated clients, UI badge, docs, and regression coverage. Oyatie was used read-only for audit/evidence/version-lineage discipline.

goals.title: Policy studio — PBAC + ABAC + RBAC
goals.objective: Add a manager/admin-customizable access policy studio that composes role grants (RBAC), attribute conditions (ABAC: group, tenant, org, department, team, position, employment status, assignment, location, device/security posture), and permission/purpose policies (PBAC: action/resource/purpose/sensitive-action/passkey-step-up). The UI must support safe templates, scope preview, diff/impact review, passkey step-up for publishing, audit logging, rollback, and guardrails so managers can only manage policies inside their delegated authority.
goals.evidence: User clarification 2026-06-26: essentially this is PBAC, ABAC, RBAC; these should be customizable within the UI/UX by managers with necessary permissions.

Reuse generated APIs and reputable MIT/Apache dependencies only where commodity UI/protocol work should be adopted; own product flows, policy checks, auditability, tests, and native integration.

goals.title: Policy studio — PBAC + ABAC + RBAC
goals.objective: Add a manager/admin-customizable access policy studio that composes role grants (RBAC), attribute conditions (ABAC: group, tenant, org, department, team, position, employment status, assignment, location, device/security posture), and permission/purpose policies (PBAC: action/resource/purpose/sensitive-action/passkey-step-up). The UI must support safe templates, scope preview, diff/impact review, passkey step-up for publishing, audit logging, rollback, and guardrails so managers can only manage policies inside their delegated authority.
goals.evidence: User clarification 2026-06-26: essentially this is PBAC, ABAC, RBAC; these should be customizable within the UI/UX by managers with necessary permissions.

goals.title: Native mobile collaboration + approvals
goals.objective: Polish the iOS Swift and Android Kotlin employee phone apps around today work/clock-in, passkey signing and sensitive-action authorization, notifications and alerts, approvals such as overtime, messenger, company mail, personal/shared calendar, and polls. Reuse generated APIs and reputable MIT/Apache dependencies only where commodity UI/protocol work should be adopted; own product flows, policy checks, auditability, tests, and native integration.
goals.evidence: User 2026-06-26: not just Swift but Kotlin for Android; phone app primary for clock-in, passkey auth/signing/authorization, notifications/alerts, approvals, messenger, mail, calendar, polls; end product must be well integrated and interact as if native.

goals.title: Policy studio — PBAC + ABAC + RBAC
goals.objective: Add a manager/admin-customizable access policy studio that composes role grants (RBAC), attribute conditions (ABAC: group, tenant, org, department, team, position, employment status, assignment, location, device/security posture), and permission/purpose policies (PBAC: action/resource/purpose/sensitive-action/passkey-step-up). The UI must support safe templates, scope preview, diff/impact review, passkey step-up for publishing, audit logging, rollback, and guardrails so managers can only manage policies inside their delegated authority.
goals.evidence: User clarification 2026-06-26: essentially this is PBAC, ABAC, RBAC; these should be customizable within the UI/UX by managers with necessary permissions.

goals.title: Collaboration suite — messenger, mail, calendar, polls
goals.objective: Polish and wire an enterprise collaboration suite: messenger with support escalation/action cards/SLA context; mail inbox/thread workflow beyond settings; calendar with personal work calendar plus shared team/department/org/group calendars, availability, work-order/leave/approval overlays, and conflict visibility; polls/surveys with role-scoped audience targeting, anonymity controls, deadlines, reminders, results, and audit/compliance boundaries. UI must be role-aware, accessible, tested, and safe when backend support is staged.
goals.evidence: User requirement 2026-06-26: polish messenger, mail, calendar, and poll system; calendar has to be both shared(team/org/department etc) + personal(work) calendar.

goals.title: Native mobile collaboration + approvals
goals.objective: Polish the iOS Swift and Android Kotlin employee phone apps around today work/clock-in, passkey signing and sensitive-action authorization, notifications and alerts, approvals such as overtime, messenger, company mail, personal/shared calendar, and polls. Reuse generated APIs and reputable MIT/Apache dependencies only where commodity UI/protocol work should be adopted; own product flows, policy checks, auditability, tests, and native integration.
goals.evidence: User 2026-06-26: not just Swift but Kotlin for Android; phone app primary for clock-in, passkey auth/signing/authorization, notifications/alerts, approvals, messenger, mail, calendar, polls; end product must be well integrated and interact as if native.

goals.title: Track A — Stabilize live ops
goals.objective: Fix issue-#19 broken-loop flows (#40 batches) + remaining #19; every flow create→admin-visible round-trip tested as real mnt_rt
goals.evidence: Active aggregate /goal in .omc/ultragoal/plans/conglomerate-platform/goals.json + ledger.jsonl covers G001-G015; marking story G001-track-a-stabilize-live-ops COMPLETE (G002-G015 remain active). IMPLEMENTATION: #19 core flows fixed+deployed (#19.13 mgmt_no normalize, #19.17 daily-plan visibility, #19.18 purchase create WORM-defer + error surfacing, #19.21 inquiry org-scope/storefront_org, #19.22 PM-schedule); codex P0 authz (OrgWideQueueTriage replaces is_admin_like widen; daily-plan gate; mgmt_no exact-then-unique binding+preview). Commits 3a87533..0755965. VALIDATION/REVIEW: review-gate (correctness+RLS-as-mnt_rt+codex cross-model+web) = GO-with-fixes, 2 mediums landed; security-reviewer on RBAC sub-spec (separate goal); real mnt_rt tests; backend gates PASSED; full workspace cargo test green after org_setup pagination fix. DEPLOYED LIVE: blue-green /readyz-smoke promotion to mnt-app@804d601+mnt-web@a28c85d, rollouts Healthy, live triager diagnostic done (1/9 admins low-risk). Blocked-external: #19.14 FLMS (Windows MSI). Orthogonal pre-existing CI debt (api-clients/E2E/Android) = task #47.

evidence: Used Oyatie read-only references for workflow policy preview rollups and policy version diff impact reports. Implemented POST /api/v1/policy/roles/{id}/status-preview with RoleManage authorization, delegated-scope guard reuse, no mutation/passkey requirement, assignment count under tenant RLS, and deterministic warnings. Policy Studio now calls the preview before WebAuthn step-up and renders a Korean role-status impact panel without exposing raw role IDs. OpenAPI and generated TS/Kotlin/Swift clients include previewPolicyRoleStatus and PolicyRoleStatusPreviewResponse. REST tests assert preview current/requested status, permission/condition counts, planned_assignment_count, requires_passkey_step_up=true, effective_runtime_change=false, and warnings; web tests assert preview renders before the patch and patch waits for passkey. Verification: cd backend && cargo fmt --all -- --check passed; cargo metadata reported packages=75 workspace_members=75; npm run gen:api passed with existing nullable/OpenAPI 3.1 warnings; generated-client grep found previewPolicyRoleStatus and PolicyRoleStatusPreviewResponse in TS/Kotlin/Swift; npm run check:ts -- --pretty false passed; npm --workspace web run test -- src/pages/PolicyStudioPage.test.tsx passed 6/6; npm --workspace web run lint passed; npm --workspace web run build passed; npm run check:kotlin passed; npm run check:swift passed; Docker rust:1.96 reported rustc 1.96.0 and cargo test -p mnt-identity-rest --test org_setup passed 17/17; git diff --check passed; Docker cargo caches removed; no leftover host cargo/rustc processes. Validation gap: bounded host npm run check:openapi-app timed out after 75s while compiling mnt-identity-adapter-postgres, repeating the known host rustc hang pattern rather than producing a code error.
message: P1k role status impact preview: publishing, rolling back, or retiring a custom role now performs a read-only impact preview before passkey step-up, showing before/after status, policy surface size, planned-assignment count, staged-runtime warnings, and preserving the sensitive action passkey gate.

after.evidence: Used Oyatie read-only references for workflow policy preview rollups and policy version diff impact reports. Implemented POST /api/v1/policy/roles/{id}/status-preview with RoleManage authorization, delegated-scope guard reuse, no mutation/passkey requirement, assignment count under tenant RLS, and deterministic warnings. Policy Studio now calls the preview before WebAuthn step-up and renders a Korean role-status impact panel without exposing raw role IDs. OpenAPI and generated TS/Kotlin/Swift clients include previewPolicyRoleStatus and PolicyRoleStatusPreviewResponse. REST tests assert preview current/requested status, permission/condition counts, planned_assignment_count, requires_passkey_step_up=true, effective_runtime_change=false, and warnings; web tests assert preview renders before the patch and patch waits for passkey. Verification: cd backend && cargo fmt --all -- --check passed; cargo metadata reported packages=75 workspace_members=75; npm run gen:api passed with existing nullable/OpenAPI 3.1 warnings; generated-client grep found previewPolicyRoleStatus and PolicyRoleStatusPreviewResponse in TS/Kotlin/Swift; npm run check:ts -- --pretty false passed; npm --workspace web run test -- src/pages/PolicyStudioPage.test.tsx passed 6/6; npm --workspace web run lint passed; npm --workspace web run build passed; npm run check:kotlin passed; npm run check:swift passed; Docker rust:1.96 reported rustc 1.96.0 and cargo test -p mnt-identity-rest --test org_setup passed 17/17; git diff --check passed; Docker cargo caches removed; no leftover host cargo/rustc processes. Validation gap: bounded host npm run check:openapi-app timed out after 75s while compiling mnt-identity-adapter-postgres, repeating the known host rustc hang pattern rather than producing a code error.
after.rationale: P1k role status impact preview: publishing, rolling back, or retiring a custom role now performs a read-only impact preview before passkey step-up, showing before/after status, policy surface size, planned-assignment count, staged-runtime warnings, and preserving the sensitive action passkey gate.

goals.title: Policy studio — PBAC + ABAC + RBAC
goals.objective: Add a manager/admin-customizable access policy studio that composes role grants (RBAC), attribute conditions (ABAC: group, tenant, org, department, team, position, employment status, assignment, location, device/security posture), and permission/purpose policies (PBAC: action/resource/purpose/sensitive-action/passkey-step-up). The UI must support safe templates, scope preview, diff/impact review, passkey step-up for publishing, audit logging, rollback, and guardrails so managers can only manage policies inside their delegated authority.
goals.evidence: User clarification 2026-06-26: essentially this is PBAC, ABAC, RBAC; these should be customizable within the UI/UX by managers with necessary permissions.

goals.objective: ObjectLink + back-stack breadcrumb + Cmd-K command palette + shared DataTable

goals.title: Collaboration suite — messenger, mail, calendar, polls
goals.objective: Polish and wire an enterprise collaboration suite: messenger with support escalation/action cards/SLA context; mail inbox/thread workflow beyond settings; calendar with personal work calendar plus shared team/department/org/group calendars, availability, work-order/leave/approval overlays, and conflict visibility; polls/surveys with role-scoped audience targeting, anonymity controls, deadlines, reminders, results, and audit/compliance boundaries. UI must be role-aware, accessible, tested, and safe when backend support is staged.
goals.evidence: User requirement 2026-06-26: polish messenger, mail, calendar, and poll system; calendar has to be both shared(team/org/department etc) + personal(work) calendar.

Reuse generated APIs and reputable MIT/Apache dependencies only where commodity UI/protocol work should be adopted; own product flows, policy checks, auditability, tests, and native integration.

goals.title: Policy studio — PBAC + ABAC + RBAC
goals.objective: Add a manager/admin-customizable access policy studio that composes role grants (RBAC), attribute conditions (ABAC: group, tenant, org, department, team, position, employment status, assignment, location, device/security posture), and permission/purpose policies (PBAC: action/resource/purpose/sensitive-action/passkey-step-up). The UI must support safe templates, scope preview, diff/impact review, passkey step-up for publishing, audit logging, rollback, and guardrails so managers can only manage policies inside their delegated authority.
goals.evidence: User clarification 2026-06-26: essentially this is PBAC, ABAC, RBAC; these should be customizable within the UI/UX by managers with necessary permissions.

Reuse generated APIs and reputable MIT/Apache dependencies only where commodity UI/protocol work should be adopted; own product flows, policy checks, auditability, tests, and native integration.

Policy Studio now edits custom roles from the catalog form, locks role key, saves with passkey, refreshes catalog/audit, and labels role update audit evidence. Generated TS/Kotlin/Swift clients include UpdatePolicyRoleRequest/updatePolicyRole. Verification: backend cargo fmt --check; cargo metadata packages=75 workspace_members=75; npm run gen:api; npm run check:ts -- --pretty false; npm --workspace web run test -- src/pages/PolicyStudioPage.test.tsx (7 passed); npm --workspace web run lint; npm --workspace web run build; npm run check:kotlin; npm run check:swift; Docker rust:1.96 cargo test -p mnt-identity-rest --test org_setup -- --nocapture (18 passed); git diff --check; Docker cargo caches cleaned. Known host gap: npm run check:openapi-app timed out at 75s compiling identity application/adapter-postgres, no leftover processes.
after.rationale: P1l custom-role definition editing: RoleManage holders can now edit tenant custom-role metadata, permissions, and ABAC/PBAC conditions through a passkey-gated audited path while keeping role keys/status immutable and runtime authorization staged.

evidence: P1m runtime effective custom-role resolver: ACTIVE non-system assignments now attach fail-closed EffectiveFeatureGrant values to the request principal via mnt-platform-request-context; authorize accepts those grants only inside the already-resolved branch scope; DRAFT/RETIRED ignored; unsupported ABAC/PBAC conditions fail closed; RoleManage, ElevatedRoleGrant, and OrgWideQueueTriage remain system-role-only. Verification: Docker Rust 1.96 authz policy 18 passed; identity org_setup 18 passed; cargo check --workspace --all-targets passed; cargo fmt --check passed; cargo metadata packages=75 workspace_members=75; npm check:ts passed; PolicyStudio test 7 passed; web lint/build passed; git diff --check passed; check:openapi-app bounded 120s timeout while compiling with no leftover processes.
message: P1m runtime-effective custom-role resolver: ACTIVE custom-role assignments now contribute ordinary feature grants to the central request principal without widening branch/group/platform scope; unsupported conditions and elevated/scope-widening features fail closed.

goals.title: Policy studio — PBAC + ABAC + RBAC
goals.objective: Add a manager/admin-customizable access policy studio that composes role grants (RBAC), attribute conditions (ABAC: group, tenant, org, department, team, position, employment status, assignment, location, device/security posture), and permission/purpose policies (PBAC: action/resource/purpose/sensitive-action/passkey-step-up). The UI must support safe templates, scope preview, diff/impact review, passkey step-up for publishing, audit logging, rollback, and guardrails so managers can only manage policies inside their delegated authority.
goals.evidence: User clarification 2026-06-26: essentially this is PBAC, ABAC, RBAC; these should be customizable within the UI/UX by managers with necessary permissions.

goals.title: Collaboration suite — messenger, mail, calendar, polls
goals.objective: Polish and wire an enterprise collaboration suite: messenger with support escalation/action cards/SLA context; mail inbox/thread workflow beyond settings; calendar with personal work calendar plus shared team/department/org/group calendars, availability, work-order/leave/approval overlays, and conflict visibility; polls/surveys with role-scoped audience targeting, anonymity controls, deadlines, reminders, results, and audit/compliance boundaries. UI must be role-aware, accessible, tested, and safe when backend support is staged.
goals.evidence: User requirement 2026-06-26: polish messenger, mail, calendar, and poll system; calendar has to be both shared(team/org/department etc) + personal(work) calendar.

goals.title: Native mobile collaboration + approvals
goals.objective: Polish the iOS Swift and Android Kotlin employee phone apps around today work/clock-in, passkey signing and sensitive-action authorization, notifications and alerts, approvals such as overtime, messenger, company mail, personal/shared calendar, and polls. Reuse generated APIs and reputable MIT/Apache dependencies only where commodity UI/protocol work should be adopted; own product flows, policy checks, auditability, tests, and native integration.
goals.evidence: User 2026-06-26: not just Swift but Kotlin for Android; phone app primary for clock-in, passkey auth/signing/authorization, notifications/alerts, approvals, messenger, mail, calendar, polls; end product must be well integrated and interact as if native.

goals.title: P2 — Equipment Object View
goals.objective: /equipment/:id via scaffold + links rail + actions; closes imported-equipment-not-viewable (#8)
goals.evidence: G005 complete: added branch-scoped GET /api/v1/equipment/{id} for imported equipment object links, with EquipmentReadQuery, PgRegistryStore::get_equipment under with_org_conn/RLS, REST get_equipment read authorization, OpenAPI getEquipment, and generated TS/Kotlin/Swift clients. after.evidence: P1m runtime effective custom-role resolver: ACTIVE non-system assignments now attach fail-closed EffectiveFeatureGrant values to the request principal via mnt-platform-request-context; authorize accepts those grants only inside the already-resolved branch scope; DRAFT/RETIRED ignored; unsupported ABAC/PBAC conditions fail closed; RoleManage, ElevatedRoleGrant, and OrgWideQueueTriage remain system-role-only. Verification: Docker Rust 1.96 authz policy 18 passed; identity org_setup 18 passed; cargo check --workspace --all-targets passed; cargo fmt --check passed; cargo metadata packages=75 workspace_members=75; npm check:ts passed; PolicyStudio test 7 passed; web lint/build passed; git diff --check passed; check:openapi-app bounded 120s timeout while compiling with no leftover processes.
after.rationale: P1m runtime-effective custom-role resolver: ACTIVE custom-role assignments now contribute ordinary feature grants to the central request principal without widening branch/group/platform scope; unsupported conditions and elevated/scope-widening features fail closed.

evidence: P1n G016 hardening: assignment preview effective flag now reflects whether requested saved assignments include at least one ACTIVE custom role that will produce runtime grants for the target live branch scope; DRAFT/RETIRED, unsupported conditions, invalid branch ids/operators, and reserved elevated/scope-widening features stay fail-closed. REST now treats OrgWideQueueTriage as reserved alongside RoleManage/ElevatedRoleGrant for custom role definition/catalog hiding. Delegated branch-scoped policy managers are proven unable to mutate out-of-scope assignment writes before passkey; in-scope writes still require passkey. OpenAPI and generated TS/Kotlin/Swift clients regenerated to remove stale planned/future-auth contract text. Oyatie was used read-only for PDP/versioned fail-closed publish/apply patterns; no Oyatie files were edited. Verification: cargo fmt --check passed; Docker Rust 1.96 authz policy 18 passed; Docker Rust 1.96 identity org_setup 19 passed; Docker Rust 1.96 cargo check --workspace --all-targets passed; npm run check:ts passed; PolicyStudioPage test 7 passed; npm run check:kotlin passed; npm run check:swift passed; web lint/build passed; cargo metadata packages=75 workspace_members=75; git diff --check passed; stale contract rg no matches; check:openapi-app bounded 240s timeout while host cargo was still compiling changed crates, no leftover processes.
message: P1n contract-hardening after runtime-effective custom-role resolver: make Policy Studio preview/API contracts reflect live ACTIVE assignment behavior, reject scope-widening custom role definitions at REST, and lock fail-closed condition/delegated-assignment behavior with regressions.

goals.title: Policy studio — PBAC + ABAC + RBAC
goals.objective: Add a manager/admin-customizable access policy studio that composes role grants (RBAC), attribute conditions (ABAC: group, tenant, org, department, team, position, employment status, assignment, location, device/security posture), and permission/purpose policies (PBAC: action/resource/purpose/sensitive-action/passkey-step-up). The UI must support safe templates, scope preview, diff/impact review, passkey step-up for publishing, audit logging, rollback, and guardrails so managers can only manage policies inside their delegated authority.
goals.evidence: User clarification 2026-06-26: essentially this is PBAC, ABAC, RBAC; these should be customizable within the UI/UX by managers with necessary permissions.

goals.title: Collaboration suite — messenger, mail, calendar, polls
goals.objective: Polish and wire an enterprise collaboration suite: messenger with support escalation/action cards/SLA context; mail inbox/thread workflow beyond settings; calendar with personal work calendar plus shared team/department/org/group calendars, availability, work-order/leave/approval overlays, and conflict visibility; polls/surveys with role-scoped audience targeting, anonymity controls, deadlines, reminders, results, and audit/compliance boundaries. UI must be role-aware, accessible, tested, and safe when backend support is staged.
goals.evidence: User requirement 2026-06-26: polish messenger, mail, calendar, and poll system; calendar has to be both shared(team/org/department etc) + personal(work) calendar.

goals.title: Native mobile collaboration + approvals
goals.objective: Polish the iOS Swift and Android Kotlin employee phone apps around today work/clock-in, passkey signing and sensitive-action authorization, notifications and alerts, approvals such as overtime, messenger, company mail, personal/shared calendar, and polls. Reuse generated APIs and reputable MIT/Apache dependencies only where commodity UI/protocol work should be adopted; own product flows, policy checks, auditability, tests, and native integration.
goals.evidence: User 2026-06-26: not just Swift but Kotlin for Android; phone app primary for clock-in, passkey auth/signing/authorization, notifications/alerts, approvals, messenger, mail, calendar, polls; end product must be well integrated and interact as if native.

goals.title: Track A — Stabilize live ops
goals.objective: Fix issue-#19 broken-loop flows (#40 batches) + remaining #19; every flow create→admin-visible round-trip tested as real mnt_rt
goals.evidence: Active aggregate /goal in .omc/ultragoal/plans/conglomerate-platform/goals.json + ledger.jsonl covers G001-G015; marking story G001-track-a-stabilize-live-ops COMPLETE (G002-G015 remain active). IMPLEMENTATION: #19 core flows fixed+deployed (#19.13 mgmt_no normalize, #19.17 daily-plan visibility, #19.18 purchase create WORM-defer + error surfacing, #19.21 inquiry org-scope/storefront_org, #19.22 PM-schedule); codex P0 authz (OrgWideQueueTriage replaces is_admin_like widen; daily-plan gate; mgmt_no exact-then-unique binding+preview). Commits 3a87533..0755965. VALIDATION/REVIEW: review-gate (correctness+RLS-as-mnt_rt+codex cross-model+web) = GO-with-fixes, 2 mediums landed; security-reviewer on RBAC sub-spec (separate goal); real mnt_rt tests; backend gates PASSED; full workspace cargo test green after org_setup pagination fix. DEPLOYED LIVE: blue-green /readyz-smoke promotion to mnt-app@804d601+mnt-web@a28c85d, rollouts Healthy, live triager diagnostic done (1/9 admins low-risk). Blocked-external: #19.14 FLMS (Windows MSI). Orthogonal pre-existing CI debt (api-clients/E2E/Android) = task #47.

after.evidence: P1n G016 hardening: assignment preview effective flag now reflects whether requested saved assignments include at least one ACTIVE custom role that will produce runtime grants for the target live branch scope; DRAFT/RETIRED, unsupported conditions, invalid branch ids/operators, and reserved elevated/scope-widening features stay fail-closed. REST now treats OrgWideQueueTriage as reserved alongside RoleManage/ElevatedRoleGrant for custom role definition/catalog hiding. Delegated branch-scoped policy managers are proven unable to mutate out-of-scope assignment writes before passkey; in-scope writes still require passkey. OpenAPI and generated TS/Kotlin/Swift clients regenerated to remove stale planned/future-auth contract text. Oyatie was used read-only for PDP/versioned fail-closed publish/apply patterns; no Oyatie files were edited. Verification: cargo fmt --check passed; Docker Rust 1.96 authz policy 18 passed; Docker Rust 1.96 identity org_setup 19 passed; Docker Rust 1.96 cargo check --workspace --all-targets passed; npm run check:ts passed; PolicyStudioPage test 7 passed; npm run check:kotlin passed; npm run check:swift passed; web lint/build passed; cargo metadata packages=75 workspace_members=75; git diff --check passed; stale contract rg no matches; check:openapi-app bounded 240s timeout while host cargo was still compiling changed crates, no leftover processes.
after.rationale: P1n contract-hardening after runtime-effective custom-role resolver: make Policy Studio preview/API contracts reflect live ACTIVE assignment behavior, reject scope-widening custom role definitions at REST, and lock fail-closed condition/delegated-assignment behavior with regressions.

evidence: G016 P1o complete: Policy Studio status preview/update now allows only DRAFT->ACTIVE, ACTIVE->DRAFT, and ACTIVE->RETIRED; RETIRED is terminal. Invalid lifecycle transitions are rejected before passkey and backed by a Postgres adapter defense-in-depth check. Rollback previews now warn that assigned ACTIVE grants will stop at runtime; the console status preview displays the effective runtime impact boolean and has Korean copy for rollback/retire/publish effects. OpenAPI documents the lifecycle state machine and generated TS/Kotlin/Swift clients were regenerated. Oyatie was used read-only for versioned publish/apply/fail-closed policy patterns; no Oyatie files were edited. Verification: cargo fmt --check passed; Docker Rust 1.96 + Postgres 18.4 SQLx full mnt-identity-rest org_setup test passed 20/20; Docker Rust 1.96 cargo check --workspace --all-targets passed with repo root mounted for docs/reference include_bytes; npm run check:ts passed; PolicyStudioPage test passed 8/8; npm run check:kotlin BUILD SUCCESSFUL; npm run check:swift passed; web lint/build passed; cargo metadata packages=75 workspace_members=75; git diff --check passed; stale contract grep found only new lifecycle strings. check:openapi-app remained a bounded smoke gap: 240s wrapper timed out while host cargo was still compiling mnt-app crates; orphaned processes were stopped.
message: P1o status lifecycle hardening: make custom-role publish/rollback/retire transitions fail-closed, terminal RETIRED non-resurrectable, and console preview explicit about runtime impact before passkey.

goals.title: Policy studio — PBAC + ABAC + RBAC
goals.objective: Add a manager/admin-customizable access policy studio that composes role grants (RBAC), attribute conditions (ABAC: group, tenant, org, department, team, position, employment status, assignment, location, device/security posture), and permission/purpose policies (PBAC: action/resource/purpose/sensitive-action/passkey-step-up). The UI must support safe templates, scope preview, diff/impact review, passkey step-up for publishing, audit logging, rollback, and guardrails so managers can only manage policies inside their delegated authority.
goals.evidence: User clarification 2026-06-26: essentially this is PBAC, ABAC, RBAC; these should be customizable within the UI/UX by managers with necessary permissions.

goals.title: Collaboration suite — messenger, mail, calendar, polls
goals.objective: Polish and wire an enterprise collaboration suite: messenger with support escalation/action cards/SLA context; mail inbox/thread workflow beyond settings; calendar with personal work calendar plus shared team/department/org/group calendars, availability, work-order/leave/approval overlays, and conflict visibility; polls/surveys with role-scoped audience targeting, anonymity controls, deadlines, reminders, results, and audit/compliance boundaries. UI must be role-aware, accessible, tested, and safe when backend support is staged.
goals.evidence: User requirement 2026-06-26: polish messenger, mail, calendar, and poll system; calendar has to be both shared(team/org/department etc) + personal(work) calendar.

goals.title: Native mobile collaboration + approvals
goals.objective: Polish the iOS Swift and Android Kotlin employee phone apps around today work/clock-in, passkey signing and sensitive-action authorization, notifications and alerts, approvals such as overtime, messenger, company mail, personal/shared calendar, and polls. Reuse generated APIs and reputable MIT/Apache dependencies only where commodity UI/protocol work should be adopted; own product flows, policy checks, auditability, tests, and native integration.
goals.evidence: User 2026-06-26: not just Swift but Kotlin for Android; phone app primary for clock-in, passkey auth/signing/authorization, notifications/alerts, approvals, messenger, mail, calendar, polls; end product must be well integrated and interact as if native.

goals.title: Native mobile collaboration + approvals
goals.objective: Polish the iOS Swift and Android Kotlin employee phone apps around today work/clock-in, passkey signing and sensitive-action authorization, notifications and alerts, approvals such as overtime, messenger, company mail, personal/shared calendar, and polls. Reuse generated APIs and reputable MIT/Apache dependencies only where commodity UI/protocol work should be adopted; own product flows, policy checks, auditability, tests, and native integration.
