after.evidence: G016 P1o complete: Policy Studio status preview/update now allows only DRAFT->ACTIVE, ACTIVE->DRAFT, and ACTIVE->RETIRED; RETIRED is terminal. Invalid lifecycle transitions are rejected before passkey and backed by a Postgres adapter defense-in-depth check. Rollback previews now warn that assigned ACTIVE grants will stop at runtime; the console status preview displays the effective runtime impact boolean and has Korean copy for rollback/retire/publish effects. OpenAPI documents the lifecycle state machine and generated TS/Kotlin/Swift clients were regenerated. Oyatie was used read-only for versioned publish/apply/fail-closed policy patterns; no Oyatie files were edited. Verification: cargo fmt --check passed; Docker Rust 1.96 + Postgres 18.4 SQLx full mnt-identity-rest org_setup test passed 20/20; Docker Rust 1.96 cargo check --workspace --all-targets passed with repo root mounted for docs/reference include_bytes; npm run check:ts passed; PolicyStudioPage test passed 8/8; npm run check:kotlin BUILD SUCCESSFUL; npm run check:swift passed; web lint/build passed; cargo metadata packages=75 workspace_members=75; git diff --check passed; stale contract grep found only new lifecycle strings. check:openapi-app remained a bounded smoke gap: 240s wrapper timed out while host cargo was still compiling mnt-app crates; orphaned processes were stopped.
after.rationale: P1o status lifecycle hardening: make custom-role publish/rollback/retire transitions fail-closed, terminal RETIRED non-resurrectable, and console preview explicit about runtime impact before passkey.

evidence: G016 P1p complete: assignment preview now returns per-custom-role runtime_effective and runtime_warnings, adds deterministic preview-level fail-closed warning codes, and includes custom-role feature grants only when the role is ACTIVE, conditions narrow to the target user's live branch scope, and permissions are runtime-supported. DRAFT roles, unsupported ABAC/PBAC conditions, invalid branch values, branch-scope mismatches, and roles with no supported permissions remain planning/audit-only with explicit reasons. Policy Studio UI now renders a Korean 런타임 판정 panel for planning/audit-only custom roles and no longer shows misleading custom grants for fail-closed roles. OpenAPI documents the per-role runtime decision and generated TS/Kotlin/Swift clients were regenerated. Oyatie was used read-only for deterministic policy-preview rationale and service-owned authz patterns; no Oyatie files were edited. Verification: cargo fmt --check passed; Docker Rust 1.96 + Postgres 18.4 SQLx targeted assignment-preview test passed; Docker Rust 1.96 + Postgres 18.4 full mnt-identity-rest org_setup test passed 20/20; Docker Rust 1.96 cargo check --workspace --all-targets passed; npm run check:ts passed; PolicyStudioPage test passed 8/8; web lint passed; web build passed; npm run check:kotlin BUILD SUCCESSFUL; npm run check:swift passed; cargo metadata packages=75 workspace_members=75; git diff --check passed; Oyatie touched-file status clean. check:openapi-app remained a bounded smoke gap: 240s wrapper timed out while host cargo was compiling mnt-app crates; orphaned processes and temp Docker/Postgres state were cleaned.
message: P1p assignment preview runtime-rationale hardening: make Policy Studio previews fail-closed and explain when custom roles are planning/audit-only instead of falsely presenting grants.

goals.title: Policy studio — PBAC + ABAC + RBAC
goals.objective: Add a manager/admin-customizable access policy studio that composes role grants (RBAC), attribute conditions (ABAC: group, tenant, org, department, team, position, employment status, assignment, location, device/security posture), and permission/purpose policies (PBAC: action/resource/purpose/sensitive-action/passkey-step-up). The UI must support safe templates, scope preview, diff/impact review, passkey step-up for publishing, audit logging, rollback, and guardrails so managers can only manage policies inside their delegated authority.
goals.evidence: User clarification 2026-06-26: essentially this is PBAC, ABAC, RBAC; these should be customizable within the UI/UX by managers with necessary permissions.

goals.title: Policy studio — PBAC + ABAC + RBAC
goals.objective: Add a manager/admin-customizable access policy studio that composes role grants (RBAC), attribute conditions (ABAC: group, tenant, org, department, team, position, employment status, assignment, location, device/security posture), and permission/purpose policies (PBAC: action/resource/purpose/sensitive-action/passkey-step-up). The UI must support safe templates, scope preview, diff/impact review, passkey step-up for publishing, audit logging, rollback, and guardrails so managers can only manage policies inside their delegated authority.
goals.evidence: User clarification 2026-06-26: essentially this is PBAC, ABAC, RBAC; these should be customizable within the UI/UX by managers with necessary permissions.

goals.title: Collaboration suite — messenger, mail, calendar, polls
goals.objective: Polish and wire an enterprise collaboration suite: messenger with support escalation/action cards/SLA context; mail inbox/thread workflow beyond settings; calendar with personal work calendar plus shared team/department/org/group calendars, availability, work-order/leave/approval overlays, and conflict visibility; polls/surveys with role-scoped audience targeting, anonymity controls, deadlines, reminders, results, and audit/compliance boundaries. UI must be role-aware, accessible, tested, and safe when backend support is staged.
goals.evidence: User requirement 2026-06-26: polish messenger, mail, calendar, and poll system; calendar has to be both shared(team/org/department etc) + personal(work) calendar.

goals.title: Native mobile collaboration + approvals
goals.objective: Polish the iOS Swift and Android Kotlin employee phone apps around today work/clock-in, passkey signing and sensitive-action authorization, notifications and alerts, approvals such as overtime, messenger, company mail, personal/shared calendar, and polls. Reuse generated APIs and reputable MIT/Apache dependencies only where commodity UI/protocol work should be adopted; own product flows, policy checks, auditability, tests, and native integration.
goals.evidence: User 2026-06-26: not just Swift but Kotlin for Android; phone app primary for clock-in, passkey auth/signing/authorization, notifications/alerts, approvals, messenger, mail, calendar, polls; end product must be well integrated and interact as if native.

goals.title: Track A — Stabilize live ops
goals.objective: Fix issue-#19 broken-loop flows (#40 batches) + remaining #19; every flow create→admin-visible round-trip tested as real mnt_rt
goals.evidence: Active aggregate /goal in .omc/ultragoal/plans/conglomerate-platform/goals.json + ledger.jsonl covers G001-G015; marking story G001-track-a-stabilize-live-ops COMPLETE (G002-G015 remain active). IMPLEMENTATION: #19 core flows fixed+deployed (#19.13 mgmt_no normalize, #19.17 daily-plan visibility, #19.18 purchase create WORM-defer + error surfacing, #19.21 inquiry org-scope/storefront_org, #19.22 PM-schedule); codex P0 authz (OrgWideQueueTriage replaces is_admin_like widen; daily-plan gate; mgmt_no exact-then-unique binding+preview). VALIDATION/REVIEW: review-gate (correctness+RLS-as-mnt_rt+codex cross-model+web) = GO-with-fixes, 2 mediums landed; security-reviewer on RBAC sub-spec (separate goal); real mnt_rt tests; backend gates PASSED; full workspace cargo test green after org_setup pagination fix. DEPLOYED LIVE: blue-green /readyz-smoke promotion to mnt-app@804d601+mnt-web@a28c85d, rollouts Healthy, live triager diagnostic done (1/9 admins low-risk). Blocked-external: #19.14 FLMS (Windows MSI). Orthogonal pre-existing CI debt (api-clients/E2E/Android) = task #47.

goals.title: Policy studio — PBAC + ABAC + RBAC
goals.objective: Add a manager/admin-customizable access policy studio that composes role grants (RBAC), attribute conditions (ABAC: group, tenant, org, department, team, position, employment status, assignment, location, device/security posture), and permission/purpose policies (PBAC: action/resource/purpose/sensitive-action/passkey-step-up). The UI must support safe templates, scope preview, diff/impact review, passkey step-up for publishing, audit logging, rollback, and guardrails so managers can only manage policies inside their delegated authority.
goals.evidence: User clarification 2026-06-26: essentially this is PBAC, ABAC, RBAC; these should be customizable within the UI/UX by managers with necessary permissions.

goals.title: Collaboration suite — messenger, mail, calendar, polls
goals.objective: Polish and wire an enterprise collaboration suite: messenger with support escalation/action cards/SLA context; mail inbox/thread workflow beyond settings; calendar with personal work calendar plus shared team/department/org/group calendars, availability, work-order/leave/approval overlays, and conflict visibility; polls/surveys with role-scoped audience targeting, anonymity controls, deadlines, reminders, results, and audit/compliance boundaries. goals.title: Native mobile collaboration + approvals
goals.objective: Polish the iOS Swift and Android Kotlin employee phone apps around today work/clock-in, passkey signing and sensitive-action authorization, notifications and alerts, approvals such as overtime, messenger, company mail, personal/shared calendar, and polls. Reuse generated APIs and reputable MIT/Apache dependencies only where commodity UI/protocol work should be adopted; own product flows, policy checks, auditability, tests, and native integration.
goals.evidence: User 2026-06-26: not just Swift but Kotlin for Android; phone app primary for clock-in, passkey auth/signing/authorization, notifications/alerts, approvals, messenger, mail, calendar, polls; end product must be well integrated and interact as if native.

evidence: G016 P1r complete: central mnt-platform-authz effective-policy resolver now reads the target user's live team inside the RLS-armed transaction and supports team equals/in conditions using Korean DB labels plus API enum aliases (MAINTENANCE/PREVENTION/MANAGEMENT/RECEPTION). Branch conditions still only narrow live branch scope; team mismatches, null/unknown team values, not_equals operators, invalid branch values, and unsupported department/position/employment/purpose/action/resource attributes continue to fail closed and produce no runtime grants. The existing SQLx policy test now proves a matching 정비/MAINTENANCE team custom role becomes runtime-effective while a RECEPTION team role is denied; it also preserves draft, elevated-feature, branch mismatch, negative operator, invalid branch, and unsupported department fail-closed assertions. Docs/comments now state branch+team as the current runtime-supported condition boundary and explicitly defer richer data-backed ABAC/PBAC until source-of-truth attributes exist. Oyatie references were used read-only for preview-before-commit/non-bypass patterns; referenced Oyatie files remained clean. Verification passed: Docker Rust 1.96 targeted SQLx resolves_active_custom_policy_role_grants_fail_closed; Docker Rust 1.96 full mnt-platform-authz policy SQLx suite 18/18; Docker Rust 1.96 cargo clippy -p mnt-platform-authz --all-targets -- -D warnings; backend cargo fmt --all --check; git diff --check; Docker Rust 1.96 cargo check --workspace --all-targets; Docker Rust 1.96 cargo metadata packages=75 workspace_members=75. message: P1r runtime authz team-condition parity: align the central effective-policy resolver with Policy Studio assignment preview so data-backed team equals/in conditions are runtime-effective and unsupported attributes remain fail-closed.

after.evidence: G016 P1r complete: central mnt-platform-authz effective-policy resolver now reads the target user's live team inside the RLS-armed transaction and supports team equals/in conditions using Korean DB labels plus API enum aliases (MAINTENANCE/PREVENTION/MANAGEMENT/RECEPTION). Branch conditions still only narrow live branch scope; team mismatches, null/unknown team values, not_equals operators, invalid branch values, and unsupported department/position/employment/purpose/action/resource attributes continue to fail closed and produce no runtime grants. The existing SQLx policy test now proves a matching 정비/MAINTENANCE team custom role becomes runtime-effective while a RECEPTION team role is denied; it also preserves draft, elevated-feature, branch mismatch, negative operator, invalid branch, and unsupported department fail-closed assertions. Docs/comments now state branch+team as the current runtime-supported condition boundary and explicitly defer richer data-backed ABAC/PBAC until source-of-truth attributes exist. Oyatie references were used read-only for preview-before-commit/non-bypass patterns; referenced Oyatie files remained clean. Verification passed: Docker Rust 1.96 targeted SQLx resolves_active_custom_policy_role_grants_fail_closed; Docker Rust 1.96 full mnt-platform-authz policy SQLx suite 18/18; Docker Rust 1.96 cargo clippy -p mnt-platform-authz --all-targets -- -D warnings; backend cargo fmt --all --check; git diff --check; Docker Rust 1.96 cargo check --workspace --all-targets; Docker Rust 1.96 cargo metadata packages=75 workspace_members=75. Temp Docker/Rust resources cleaned.

goals.title: Policy studio — PBAC + ABAC + RBAC
goals.objective: Add a manager/admin-customizable access policy studio that composes role grants (RBAC), attribute conditions (ABAC: group, tenant, org, department, team, position, employment status, assignment, location, device/security posture), and permission/purpose policies (PBAC: action/resource/purpose/sensitive-action/passkey-step-up). The UI must support safe templates, scope preview, diff/impact review, passkey step-up for publishing, audit logging, rollback, and guardrails so managers can only manage policies inside their delegated authority.
goals.evidence: User clarification 2026-06-26: essentially this is PBAC, ABAC, RBAC; these should be customizable within the UI/UX by managers with necessary permissions.

goals.title: Collaboration suite — messenger, mail, calendar, polls
goals.objective: Polish and wire an enterprise collaboration suite: messenger with support escalation/action cards/SLA context; mail inbox/thread workflow beyond settings; calendar with personal work calendar plus shared team/department/org/group calendars, availability, work-order/leave/approval overlays, and conflict visibility; polls/surveys with role-scoped audience targeting, anonymity controls, deadlines, reminders, results, and audit/compliance boundaries. UI must be role-aware, accessible, tested, and safe when backend support is staged.
goals.evidence: User requirement 2026-06-26: polish messenger, mail, calendar, and poll system; calendar has to be both shared(team/org/department etc) + personal(work) calendar.

goals.title: Native mobile collaboration + approvals
goals.objective: Polish the iOS Swift and Android Kotlin employee phone apps around today work/clock-in, passkey signing and sensitive-action authorization, notifications and alerts, approvals such as overtime, messenger, company mail, personal/shared calendar, and polls. Reuse generated APIs and reputable MIT/Apache dependencies only where commodity UI/protocol work should be adopted; own product flows, policy checks, auditability, tests, and native integration.
goals.evidence: User 2026-06-26: not just Swift but Kotlin for Android; phone app primary for clock-in, passkey auth/signing/authorization, notifications/alerts, approvals, messenger, mail, calendar, polls; end product must be well integrated and interact as if native.

evidence: G016 P1s complete: Policy assignment replacement now requires preview_acknowledged=true server-side before passkey step-up and records rejected non-previewed saves as invalid policy-studio operations. Preview endpoint still accepts role_ids only. goals.title: Policy studio — PBAC + ABAC + RBAC
goals.objective: Add a manager/admin-customizable access policy studio that composes role grants (RBAC), attribute conditions (ABAC: group, tenant, org, department, team, position, employment status, assignment, location, device/security posture), and permission/purpose policies (PBAC: action/resource/purpose/sensitive-action/passkey-step-up). The UI must support safe templates, scope preview, diff/impact review, passkey step-up for publishing, audit logging, rollback, and guardrails so managers can only manage policies inside their delegated authority.
goals.evidence: User clarification 2026-06-26: essentially this is PBAC, ABAC, RBAC; these should be customizable within the UI/UX by managers with necessary permissions.

goals.title: Collaboration suite — messenger, mail, calendar, polls
goals.objective: Polish and wire an enterprise collaboration suite: messenger with support escalation/action cards/SLA context; mail inbox/thread workflow beyond settings; calendar with personal work calendar plus shared team/department/org/group calendars, availability, work-order/leave/approval overlays, and conflict visibility; polls/surveys with role-scoped audience targeting, anonymity controls, deadlines, reminders, results, and audit/compliance boundaries. UI must be role-aware, accessible, tested, and safe when backend support is staged.

goals.title: Native mobile collaboration + approvals
goals.objective: Polish the iOS Swift and Android Kotlin employee phone apps around today work/clock-in, passkey signing and sensitive-action authorization, notifications and alerts, approvals such as overtime, messenger, company mail, personal/shared calendar, and polls. Reuse generated APIs and reputable MIT/Apache dependencies only where commodity UI/protocol work should be adopted; own product flows, policy checks, auditability, tests, and native integration.
goals.evidence: User 2026-06-26: not just Swift but Kotlin for Android; phone app primary for clock-in, passkey auth/signing/authorization, notifications/alerts, approvals, messenger, mail, calendar, polls; end product must be well integrated and interact as if native.

after.evidence: G016 P1s complete: Policy assignment replacement now requires preview_acknowledged=true server-side before passkey step-up and records rejected non-previewed saves as invalid policy-studio operations. Preview endpoint still accepts role_ids only. Policy Studio UI now disables assignment save until a successful impact preview for the current user/role selection, resets acknowledgement whenever user/role selection or loaded assignments change, and sends preview_acknowledged=true only after preview. Korean copy and OpenAPI schema updated; TS/Kotlin/Swift clients regenerated. Oyatie was used read-only as a preview-before-commit/non-bypass reference. Verification passed: backend cargo fmt --all --check; Docker Rust 1.96 SQLX_OFFLINE targeted mnt-identity-rest policy_role_catalog_exposes_policy_version_and_assignment_writes_bump_it; Docker Rust 1.96 targeted branch_scoped_policy_managers_are_limited_to_branch_condition_scope; Docker Rust 1.96 full mnt-identity-rest org_setup suite 20/20; Docker Rust 1.96 cargo clippy -p mnt-identity-rest --all-targets -- -D warnings; npm --workspace web run test -- src/pages/PolicyStudioPage.test.tsx --run (8/8); npm run check:ts -- --pretty false; npm --workspace web run lint; npm --workspace web run build; npm run check:kotlin; npm run check:swift; Docker Rust 1.96 cargo check --workspace --all-targets with full repo mount; git diff --check; Docker Rust 1.96 cargo metadata packages=75 workspace_members=75. Validation gap: npm run check:openapi-app built mnt-app but then hung in the local Node wrapper with no cargo/mnt-app child and no listener on 127.0.0.1:18080; terminated after the useful wait (exit 143). Temporary Docker/Rust resources cleaned.
after.rationale: P1s preview-ack guard: prevent sensitive policy assignment saves from bypassing current impact preview while preserving passkey step-up and delegated-scope fail-closed checks.

evidence: G016 P1t complete: added policy_assignment_preview_receipts table with org RLS, immutable org trigger, short TTL, consumed_at, actor/user/role binding, and tenant gate classification for global feature_catalog. Preview endpoint now persists a server receipt and returns preview_receipt_id/preview_receipt_expires_at; replacement endpoint requires preview_acknowledged plus preview_receipt_id and PgOrgStore consumes the unexpired unconsumed exact-role-set receipt in the same transaction as assignment replacement and audit/version writes. Policy Studio UI only saves after successful preview with a receipt. OpenAPI and TS/Kotlin/Swift clients regenerated. Test coverage verifies missing ack/receipt rejection, step-up still required, successful save, consumed receipt reuse rejected 422, and branch-scoped manager path. Oyatie was used read-only for alignment with sensitive-action step-up, policy-as-gate, and role-projection doctrine. Verification passed: backend cargo fmt --all -- --check; Docker Rust 1.96 SQLX_OFFLINE targeted policy_role_catalog_exposes_policy_version_and_assignment_writes_bump_it; Docker Rust 1.96 targeted branch_scoped_policy_managers_are_limited_to_branch_condition_scope; Docker Rust 1.96 full mnt-identity-rest org_setup suite 20/20 after audited seed-helper fix; Docker Rust 1.96 cargo clippy -p mnt-identity-rest --all-targets -- -D warnings; Docker Rust 1.96 cargo check --workspace --all-targets with full repo mount; Docker Rust 1.96 CI gates layer-boundary, audit-coverage, migration-safety, tenant-isolation, pii-no-logs, rls-arming all PASSED; npm --workspace web run test -- src/pages/PolicyStudioPage.test.tsx --run (8/8); npm run check:ts -- --pretty false; npm --workspace web run lint; npm --workspace web run build; npm run check:kotlin; npm run check:swift; npm run check:openapi-app passed after cold build/start and app-served OpenAPI comparison (exit 0, elapsed 331.7s); git diff --check; Docker Rust 1.96 cargo metadata packages=75 workspace_members=75. No OpenAPI validation gap remains for P1t.
message: P1t server-issued preview receipt closes the client-forgeable boolean acknowledgement gap: policy assignment replacement now consumes a short-lived receipt bound to org, actor, target user, and normalized role set inside the audited write transaction, while preserving delegated-scope checks and passkey step-up.

goals.title: Policy studio — PBAC + ABAC + RBAC
goals.objective: Add a manager/admin-customizable access policy studio that composes role grants (RBAC), attribute conditions (ABAC: group, tenant, org, department, team, position, employment status, assignment, location, device/security posture), and permission/purpose policies (PBAC: action/resource/purpose/sensitive-action/passkey-step-up). The UI must support safe templates, scope preview, diff/impact review, passkey step-up for publishing, audit logging, rollback, and guardrails so managers can only manage policies inside their delegated authority.
goals.evidence: User clarification 2026-06-26: essentially this is PBAC, ABAC, RBAC; these should be customizable within the UI/UX by managers with necessary permissions.

goals.title: Collaboration suite — messenger, mail, calendar, polls
goals.objective: Polish and wire an enterprise collaboration suite: messenger with support escalation/action cards/SLA context; mail inbox/thread workflow beyond settings; calendar with personal work calendar plus shared team/department/org/group calendars, availability, work-order/leave/approval overlays, and conflict visibility; polls/surveys with role-scoped audience targeting, anonymity controls, deadlines, reminders, results, and audit/compliance boundaries. UI must be role-aware, accessible, tested, and safe when backend support is staged.
goals.evidence: User requirement 2026-06-26: polish messenger, mail, calendar, and poll system; calendar has to be both shared(team/org/department etc) + personal(work) calendar.

goals.title: Native mobile collaboration + approvals
goals.objective: Polish the iOS Swift and Android Kotlin employee phone apps around today work/clock-in, passkey signing and sensitive-action authorization, notifications and alerts, approvals such as overtime, messenger, company mail, personal/shared calendar, and polls. Reuse generated APIs and reputable MIT/Apache dependencies only where commodity UI/protocol work should be adopted; own product flows, policy checks, auditability, tests, and native integration.
goals.evidence: User 2026-06-26: not just Swift but Kotlin for Android; phone app primary for clock-in, passkey auth/signing/authorization, notifications/alerts, approvals, messenger, mail, calendar, polls; end product must be well integrated and interact as if native.

Reuse generated APIs and reputable MIT/Apache dependencies only where commodity UI/protocol work should be adopted; own product flows, policy checks, auditability, tests, and native integration.
goals.evidence: User 2026-06-26: not just Swift but Kotlin for Android; phone app primary for clock-in, passkey auth/signing/authorization, notifications/alerts, approvals, messenger, mail, calendar, polls; end product must be well integrated and interact as if native.

after.evidence: G016 P1t complete: added policy_assignment_preview_receipts table with org RLS, immutable org trigger, short TTL, consumed_at, actor/user/role binding, and tenant gate classification for global feature_catalog. Preview endpoint now persists a server receipt and returns preview_receipt_id/preview_receipt_expires_at; replacement endpoint requires preview_acknowledged plus preview_receipt_id and PgOrgStore consumes the unexpired unconsumed exact-role-set receipt in the same transaction as assignment replacement and audit/version writes. Policy Studio UI only saves after successful preview with a receipt. OpenAPI and TS/Kotlin/Swift clients regenerated. Test coverage verifies missing ack/receipt rejection, step-up still required, successful save, consumed receipt reuse rejected 422, and branch-scoped manager path. Oyatie was used read-only for alignment with sensitive-action step-up, policy-as-gate, and role-projection doctrine. Verification passed: backend cargo fmt --all -- --check; Docker Rust 1.96 SQLX_OFFLINE targeted policy_role_catalog_exposes_policy_version_and_assignment_writes_bump_it; Docker Rust 1.96 targeted branch_scoped_policy_managers_are_limited_to_branch_condition_scope; Docker Rust 1.96 full mnt-identity-rest org_setup suite 20/20 after audited seed-helper fix; Docker Rust 1.96 cargo clippy -p mnt-identity-rest --all-targets -- -D warnings; Docker Rust 1.96 cargo check --workspace --all-targets with full repo mount; Docker Rust 1.96 CI gates layer-boundary, audit-coverage, migration-safety, tenant-isolation, pii-no-logs, rls-arming all PASSED; npm --workspace web run test -- src/pages/PolicyStudioPage.test.tsx --run (8/8); npm run check:ts -- --pretty false; npm --workspace web run lint; npm --workspace web run build; npm run check:kotlin; npm run check:swift; npm run check:openapi-app passed after cold build/start and app-served OpenAPI comparison (exit 0, elapsed 331.7s); git diff --check; Docker Rust 1.96 cargo metadata packages=75 workspace_members=75. No OpenAPI validation gap remains for P1t.
after.rationale: P1t server-issued preview receipt closes the client-forgeable boolean acknowledgement gap: policy assignment replacement now consumes a short-lived receipt bound to org, actor, target user, and normalized role set inside the audited write transaction, while preserving delegated-scope checks and passkey step-up.

evidence: Implemented UI gate in web/src/pages/PolicyStudioPage.tsx: assignment preview no longer auto-acknowledges; save remains disabled until an explicit checkbox acknowledgement after the preview panel is visible; acknowledgement copy added in web/src/i18n/ko.ts; regression in web/src/pages/PolicyStudioPage.test.tsx proves save disabled after preview, enabled only after checkbox, and PUT still carries preview_acknowledged true plus preview_receipt_id and passkey step_up. Oyatie read-only reference used: specs/design-system/entity-action-policy-preview.json requires destructive/sensitive actions to execute only after a current policy preview; docs/specs/task-cedar-policy-version-diff-impact-report.md reinforces deterministic impact review before widening policy changes. Verification: initial test failed on missing explicit ack; after fix npm --workspace web run test -- src/pages/PolicyStudioPage.test.tsx --run passed 8/8; npm run check:ts -- --pretty false passed; npm --workspace web run lint passed; npm --workspace web run build passed; git diff --check passed.
message: Closes the remaining forged/implicit UI acknowledgement gap for policy role assignments and aligns the manager workflow with preview-first, explicit human review before passkey-signed sensitive action.

goals.title: Policy studio — PBAC + ABAC + RBAC
goals.objective: Add a manager/admin-customizable access policy studio that composes role grants (RBAC), attribute conditions (ABAC: group, tenant, org, department, team, position, employment status, assignment, location, device/security posture), and permission/purpose policies (PBAC: action/resource/purpose/sensitive-action/passkey-step-up). The UI must support safe templates, scope preview, diff/impact review, passkey step-up for publishing, audit logging, rollback, and guardrails so managers can only manage policies inside their delegated authority.
goals.evidence: User clarification 2026-06-26: essentially this is PBAC, ABAC, RBAC; these should be customizable within the UI/UX by managers with necessary permissions.

steering.directiveText: --kind annotate_ledger --target-goal-id G016-policy-studio-pbac-abac-rbac --title G016 P1u — explicit assignment preview acknowledgement --objective Policy Studio role assignment save requires a manager to explicitly acknowledge the server-issued impact preview before passkey step-up and assignment replacement. --evidence Implemented UI gate in web/src/pages/PolicyStudioPage.tsx: assignment preview no longer auto-acknowledges; save remains disabled until an explicit checkbox acknowledgement after the preview panel is visible; acknowledgement copy added in web/src/i18n/ko.ts; regression in web/src/pages/PolicyStudioPage.test.tsx proves save disabled after preview, enabled only after checkbox, and PUT still carries preview_acknowledged true plus preview_receipt_id and passkey step_up. Oyatie read-only reference used: specs/design-system/entity-action-policy-preview.json requires destructive/sensitive actions to execute only after a current policy preview; docs/specs/task-cedar-policy-version-diff-impact-report.md reinforces deterministic impact review before widening policy changes. Verification: initial test failed on missing explicit ack; after fix npm --workspace web run test -- src/pages/PolicyStudioPage.test.tsx --run passed 8/8; npm run check:ts -- --pretty false passed; npm --workspace web run lint passed; npm --workspace web run build passed; git diff --check passed. --rationale Closes the remaining forged/implicit UI acknowledgement gap for policy role assignments and aligns the manager workflow with preview-first, explicit human review before passkey-signed sensitive action. --idempotency-key g016-p1u-explicit-assignment-preview-ack-ui-20260627 --json

goals.title: Policy studio — PBAC + ABAC + RBAC
goals.objective: Add a manager/admin-customizable access policy studio that composes role grants (RBAC), attribute conditions (ABAC: group, tenant, org, department, team, position, employment status, assignment, location, device/security posture), and permission/purpose policies (PBAC: action/resource/purpose/sensitive-action/passkey-step-up). The UI must support safe templates, scope preview, diff/impact review, passkey step-up for publishing, audit logging, rollback, and guardrails so managers can only manage policies inside their delegated authority.
goals.evidence: User clarification 2026-06-26: essentially this is PBAC, ABAC, RBAC; these should be customizable within the UI/UX by managers with necessary permissions.

goals.title: P3 — Nav fabric
goals.objective: ObjectLink + back-stack breadcrumb + Cmd-K command palette + shared DataTable

goals.title: P4 — Triage Home
goals.objective: /home per-role what-needs-me-now queues (role-workflow backlog as a surface)

goals.objective: filter/histogram/listogram/aggregate/search-around (CAP-3) + #24 analytics tiles drill-to-act

goals.title: P6 — Timeline + graph lenses
goals.objective: Equipment lifecycle ribbon + Customer-Site-Equipment-WO graph

goals.title: Action/write-back engine
goals.objective: CAP-5 transactional audited action executor + generated forms (closed-loop)

goals.title: Track C — HR core
goals.objective: Employee/직급/조직도 (#36) + Attendance(clock-in) + Leave

goals.title: Track C — Korean payroll
goals.objective: JIT sub-spec + effective-dated rate tables + golden-case tests + 노무사 validation gate

goals.title: Track C — ERP
goals.objective: 회계 double-entry + 부가세 + 전자세금계산서 relay; 구매매입/AP; 재고; 영업매출/AR (JIT sub-specs)

goals.title: Track D — Webmail enable
goals.objective: #28 Vault key + #39 inbound hardening + webmail client UI (DOMPurify gate) + unify inquiries (#42)

goals.title: Track D — Dispatch map
goals.objective: #29 clock-in-gated live location + arrival/return markers + routing

goals.title: Collaboration suite — messenger, mail, calendar, polls
goals.objective: Polish and wire an enterprise collaboration suite: messenger with support escalation/action cards/SLA context; mail inbox/thread workflow beyond settings; calendar with personal work calendar plus shared team/department/org/group calendars, availability, work-order/leave/approval overlays, and conflict visibility; polls/surveys with role-scoped audience targeting, anonymity controls, deadlines, reminders, results, and audit/compliance boundaries. UI must be role-aware, accessible, tested, and safe when backend support is staged.
goals.evidence: User requirement 2026-06-26: polish messenger, mail, calendar, and poll system; calendar has to be both shared(team/org/department etc) + personal(work) calendar.

goals.title: Native mobile collaboration + approvals
goals.objective: Polish the iOS Swift and Android Kotlin employee phone apps around today work/clock-in, passkey signing and sensitive-action authorization, notifications and alerts, approvals such as overtime, messenger, company mail, personal/shared calendar, and polls. Reuse generated APIs and reputable MIT/Apache dependencies only where commodity UI/protocol work should be adopted; own product flows, policy checks, auditability, tests, and native integration.
goals.evidence: User 2026-06-26: not just Swift but Kotlin for Android; phone app primary for clock-in, passkey auth/signing/authorization, notifications/alerts, approvals, messenger, mail, calendar, polls; end product must be well integrated and interact as if native.

goals.title: Track A — Stabilize live ops
goals.objective: Fix issue-#19 broken-loop flows (#40 batches) + remaining #19; every flow create→admin-visible round-trip tested as real mnt_rt
goals.evidence: Active aggregate /goal in .omc/ultragoal/plans/conglomerate-platform/goals.json + ledger.jsonl covers G001-G015; marking story G001-track-a-stabilize-live-ops COMPLETE (G002-G015 remain active). IMPLEMENTATION: #19 core flows fixed+deployed (#19.13 mgmt_no normalize, #19.17 daily-plan visibility, #19.18 purchase create WORM-defer + error surfacing, #19.21 inquiry org-scope/storefront_org, #19.22 PM-schedule); codex P0 authz (OrgWideQueueTriage replaces is_admin_like widen; daily-plan gate; mgmt_no exact-then-unique binding+preview). Commits 3a87533..0755965. VALIDATION/REVIEW: review-gate (correctness+RLS-as-mnt_rt+codex cross-model+web) = GO-with-fixes, 2 mediums landed; security-reviewer on RBAC sub-spec (separate goal); real mnt_rt tests; backend gates PASSED; full workspace cargo test green after org_setup pagination fix. DEPLOYED LIVE: blue-green /readyz-smoke promotion to mnt-app@804d601+mnt-web@a28c85d, rollouts Healthy, live triager diagnostic done (1/9 admins low-risk). Blocked-external: #19.14 FLMS (Windows MSI). Orthogonal pre-existing CI debt (api-clients/E2E/Android) = task #47.

goals.title: Track B0 — Org-hierarchy security
goals.objective: Write org-hierarchy.md sub-spec + architect + security-review; AccessScope (Group→법인→branch→worksite) generalizing BranchScope + RLS-preserving consolidated reads
goals.evidence: G002 complete: docs/specs/org-hierarchy.md updated; AccessScope enforcement centralizes live DB branch-scope resolution and live-scope intersection in mnt-platform-request-context + mnt-platform-authz; ordinary tenant REST/WS routes now call shared resolver and no production path trusts claims.branches; AccessScope::Group rejected outside RLS-preserving group fan-out; JWT group_roles canonicalized to GROUP_ADMIN/GROUP_VIEWER/GROUP_FINANCE on issue+verify. Verification: git diff --check backend/docs passed; cd backend && cargo fmt --check --all passed; cargo metadata --no-deps passed; static stale-principal scan passed (none missing shared resolver, no production claims.branches); static invariant checks passed; rust-analyzer stdin parse passed for changed Rust files. Independent review: code-reviewer APPROVE no blockers; architect CLEAR. Codex usage for story goal: 925605 tokens, 35m52s.

goals.title: P0 — Blueprint tokens
goals.objective: Semantic tone/color map + Mono/Won + .console scope; re-pin priority/status/SLA color helpers (extends C1-C13)
goals.evidence: G003 complete: added .console-scoped Blueprint semantic tone variables in web/src/styles.css, new shared tone helpers in web/src/lib/semantic.ts, currency/typography primitives in web/src/lib/currency.ts + web/src/lib/format.tsx, and re-pinned work-order priority, dispatch SLA, support status/priority/SLA, and equipment status helpers to shared tone classes. Authenticated AppShell/PlatformShell now carry .console bg-console-canvas while public storefront/PublicLayout remains outside scope. Applied Mono/Won in equipment id rows, equipment detail read rows, and financial asset lifecycle money rows. Tests added/updated: semantic, format, priorityClass, SlaBadge class assertions, support-format tone assertions, equipment-format, AssetLifecycleCostPanel accessible money labels. Verification: npm --workspace web run test -- src/lib/semantic.test.ts src/lib/format.test.tsx src/lib/utils.test.ts src/features/dispatch/SlaBadge.test.tsx src/features/support/support-format.test.ts src/features/equipment/equipment-format.test.ts src/features/financial/AssetLifecycleCostPanel.test.tsx = 7 files/28 tests passed; npm --workspace web run lint passed (eslint + check-ui-strings); npm --workspace web run build passed; git diff --check for touched tracked web files passed; new-file whitespace check passed. Independent review: code-reviewer APPROVE, architect CLEAR. Codex usage for story goal: 288430 tokens, 14m45s.

goals.title: P1 — Object-View kit
goals.objective: Extract ObjectViewScaffold from WorkOrderDetail (no behavior change) + timeline/approval/evidence panels (CAP-2)
goals.evidence: G004 complete: extracted thin domain-neutral ObjectViewScaffold/ObjectViewPanel/ObjectViewProperties/ObjectViewField in web/src/features/object-view; refactored WorkOrderDetail into local identity/narrative/evidence/approval/timeline panels without changing write gates; added read-only approval_line panel with localized role/status labels and tests proving no approver UUID/raw enum leak; preserved enterprise custom-role invariant by changing roleLabel fallback to safeLabel so valid custom roles display while UUIDs remain suppressed. User enterprise-operations and dynamic-role clarifications recorded in Ultragoal ledger. Verification: npm --workspace web run test -- src/features/org/org-format.test.ts src/pages/WorkOrderDetailPage.test.tsx src/features/object-view/ObjectViewScaffold.test.tsx = 3 files/8 tests passed; npm --workspace web run test -- src/features/approvals/ApprovalQueue.test.tsx src/features/dispatch/WorkOrderList.test.tsx src/pages/DispatchPage.test.tsx src/App.test.tsx = 4 files/36 tests passed; npm --workspace web run test -- src/EmptyBackend.test.tsx = 13 tests passed standalone after cross-file MSW interference was isolated; npm --workspace web run lint passed; npm --workspace web run build passed; git diff --check passed. Independent review: code-reviewer APPROVE; architect CLEAR. Codex usage for story goal: 308044 tokens, 16m45s.

goals.title: P2 — Equipment Object View
goals.objective: /equipment/:id via scaffold + links rail + actions; closes imported-equipment-not-viewable (#8)
goals.evidence: G005 complete: added branch-scoped GET /api/v1/equipment/{id} for imported equipment object links, with EquipmentReadQuery, PgRegistryStore::get_equipment under with_org_conn/RLS, REST get_equipment read authorization, OpenAPI getEquipment, and generated TS/Kotlin/Swift clients. Web /equipment/:id now uses the shared ObjectViewScaffold and typed GET-by-id instead of scanning /equipment/list, and browse rows preserve quick-view while the action link navigates to /equipment/{id}. Dynamic/customizable PBAC/ABAC/RBAC roles remain explicitly tracked as G016; this slice did not widen static role architecture beyond existing UI edit affordance gates. Regression: added backend SQLx GET-by-id/404 test (not run because direct cargo test/check is forbidden by repo hook) and web detail/browse tests. Verification: npm --workspace web run test -- src/pages/EquipmentDetailPage.test.tsx src/pages/EquipmentBrowsePage.test.tsx src/features/equipment/EquipmentManagementPanel.test.tsx src/features/equipment/equipment-format.test.ts src/features/object-view/ObjectViewScaffold.test.tsx src/EmptyBackend.test.tsx = 6 files/25 tests passed; npm run check:ts passed; npm --workspace web run lint passed; npm --workspace web run build passed; cd backend && cargo fmt --all && cargo fmt --all -- --check passed; cargo metadata --format-version 1 --no-deps passed with 75 packages/75 workspace members; TS/Kotlin/Swift client regeneration was deterministic/no-op after generation; npm run check:kotlin BUILD SUCCESSFUL; npm run check:swift Build complete; git diff --check passed. Independent review: code-reviewer APPROVE no issues, confirmed no 2000-row list scan remains and branch/RLS boundaries preserved; architect CLEAR, confirmed minimal future-compatible contract and policy-studio gap bounded to G016. Codex story goal complete: 605046 tokens, 26m59s.

after.evidence: Implemented UI gate in web/src/pages/PolicyStudioPage.tsx: assignment preview no longer auto-acknowledges; save remains disabled until an explicit checkbox acknowledgement after the preview panel is visible; acknowledgement copy added in web/src/i18n/ko.ts; regression in web/src/pages/PolicyStudioPage.test.tsx proves save disabled after preview, enabled only after checkbox, and PUT still carries preview_acknowledged true plus preview_receipt_id and passkey step_up. Oyatie read-only reference used: specs/design-system/entity-action-policy-preview.json requires destructive/sensitive actions to execute only after a current policy preview; docs/specs/task-cedar-policy-version-diff-impact-report.md reinforces deterministic impact review before widening policy changes. Verification: initial test failed on missing explicit ack; after fix npm --workspace web run test -- src/pages/PolicyStudioPage.test.tsx --run passed 8/8; npm run check:ts -- --pretty false passed; npm --workspace web run lint passed; npm --workspace web run build passed; git diff --check passed.
after.rationale: Closes the remaining forged/implicit UI acknowledgement gap for policy role assignments and aligns the manager workflow with preview-first, explicit human review before passkey-signed sensitive action.

evidence: Implemented status preview separation in web/src/pages/PolicyStudioPage.tsx: role status actions now fetch and render impact preview only; passkey WebAuthn and PATCH status mutation run only after the manager clicks the preview confirm button; cancel clears the preview; role action remains locked while preview/confirm is pending. Korean confirm/cancel copy added in web/src/i18n/ko.ts. Regression in web/src/pages/PolicyStudioPage.test.tsx proves publish and rollback preview render first, mockAssertPasskeyStepUp is not called before explicit confirmation, PATCH is not sent while WebAuthn is pending, then PATCH carries requested status + step_up after passkey resolves. Oyatie read-only reference used: specs/design-system/entity-action-policy-preview.json and cedar policy diff/impact docs require current policy preview before sensitive/destructive action execution. Verification: initial tests failed because passkey began immediately after preview; after fix npm --workspace web run test -- src/pages/PolicyStudioPage.test.tsx --run passed 8/8; npm run check:ts -- --pretty false passed; npm --workspace web run lint passed; npm --workspace web run build passed; git diff --check passed.
message: Closes the UX/security gap where a WebAuthn prompt could interrupt impact review, making policy publish/rollback an explicit preview-confirm-sign flow.

goals.title: Policy studio — PBAC + ABAC + RBAC
goals.objective: Add a manager/admin-customizable access policy studio that composes role grants (RBAC), attribute conditions (ABAC: group, tenant, org, department, team, position, employment status, assignment, location, device/security posture), and permission/purpose policies (PBAC: action/resource/purpose/sensitive-action/passkey-step-up). The UI must support safe templates, scope preview, diff/impact review, passkey step-up for publishing, audit logging, rollback, and guardrails so managers can only manage policies inside their delegated authority.
goals.evidence: User clarification 2026-06-26: essentially this is PBAC, ABAC, RBAC; these should be customizable within the UI/UX by managers with necessary permissions.

goals.title: Collaboration suite — messenger, mail, calendar, polls
goals.objective: Polish and wire an enterprise collaboration suite: messenger with support escalation/action cards/SLA context; mail inbox/thread workflow beyond settings; calendar with personal work calendar plus shared team/department/org/group calendars, availability, work-order/leave/approval overlays, and conflict visibility; polls/surveys with role-scoped audience targeting, anonymity controls, deadlines, reminders, results, and audit/compliance boundaries. UI must be role-aware, accessible, tested, and safe when backend support is staged.
goals.evidence: User requirement 2026-06-26: polish messenger, mail, calendar, and poll system; calendar has to be both shared(team/org/department etc) + personal(work) calendar.

goals.title: Native mobile collaboration + approvals
goals.objective: Polish the iOS Swift and Android Kotlin employee phone apps around today work/clock-in, passkey signing and sensitive-action authorization, notifications and alerts, approvals such as overtime, messenger, company mail, personal/shared calendar, and polls. Reuse generated APIs and reputable MIT/Apache dependencies only where commodity UI/protocol work should be adopted; own product flows, policy checks, auditability, tests, and native integration.
goals.evidence: User 2026-06-26: not just Swift but Kotlin for Android; phone app primary for clock-in, passkey auth/signing/authorization, notifications/alerts, approvals, messenger, mail, calendar, polls; end product must be well integrated and interact as if native.

goals.title: Track A — Stabilize live ops

goals.evidence: Active aggregate /goal in .omc/ultragoal/plans/conglomerate-platform/goals.json + ledger.jsonl covers G001-G015; marking story G001-track-a-stabilize-live-ops COMPLETE (G002-G015 remain active). IMPLEMENTATION: #19 core flows fixed+deployed (#19.13 mgmt_no normalize, #19.17 daily-plan visibility, #19.18 purchase create WORM-defer + error surfacing, #19.21 inquiry org-scope/storefront_org, #19.22 PM-schedule); codex P0 authz (OrgWideQueueTriage replaces is_admin_like widen; daily-plan gate; mgmt_no exact-then-unique binding+preview). goals.title: Policy studio — PBAC + ABAC + RBAC
goals.objective: Add a manager/admin-customizable access policy studio that composes role grants (RBAC), attribute conditions (ABAC: group, tenant, org, department, team, position, employment status, assignment, location, device/security posture), and permission/purpose policies (PBAC: action/resource/purpose/sensitive-action/passkey-step-up). The UI must support safe templates, scope preview, diff/impact review, passkey step-up for publishing, audit logging, rollback, and guardrails so managers can only manage policies inside their delegated authority.
goals.evidence: User clarification 2026-06-26: essentially this is PBAC, ABAC, RBAC; these should be customizable within the UI/UX by managers with necessary permissions.

goals.title: Native mobile collaboration + approvals
goals.objective: Polish the iOS Swift and Android Kotlin employee phone apps around today work/clock-in, passkey signing and sensitive-action authorization, notifications and alerts, approvals such as overtime, messenger, company mail, personal/shared calendar, and polls. Reuse generated APIs and reputable MIT/Apache dependencies only where commodity UI/protocol work should be adopted; own product flows, policy checks, auditability, tests, and native integration.
goals.evidence: User 2026-06-26: not just Swift but Kotlin for Android; phone app primary for clock-in, passkey auth/signing/authorization, notifications/alerts, approvals, messenger, mail, calendar, polls; end product must be well integrated and interact as if native.

evidence: Used Oyatie read-only references for pre-execution policy preview/autonomy ceiling and deterministic policy impact discipline. Added backend tests first in backend/crates/identity/rest/src/lib.rs: assignment_ceiling_rejects_capability_actor_does_not_hold, assignment_ceiling_requires_custom_grant_scope_to_cover_role_scope, and assignment_ceiling_requires_elevated_grant_for_user_management_roles. Red evidence: Docker Rust 1.96 cargo test -p mnt-identity-rest policy_role_template_tests::assignment_ceiling_rejects_capability_actor_does_not_hold --lib failed with missing ensure_policy_roles_inside_actor_permission_ceiling. Implemented server-side guard wired into assignment preview and replace paths: requested role branch reach is computed from branch conditions, each non-deny permission must be held by the principal through system roles or runtime effective grants covering that scope, elevated/scope-widening custom features remain rejected, and user_manage assignment requires ElevatedRoleGrant. Verification: Docker Rust 1.96 cargo fmt --all -- --check passed; cargo test -p mnt-identity-rest --lib passed 5/5; cargo clippy -p mnt-identity-rest --all-targets -- -D warnings passed; disposable Postgres org_setup integration passed 20/20; git diff --check passed.
message: Closes the R2 grant-less-than-or-equal-self assignment choke point before expanding manager-customizable PBAC/ABAC/RBAC; prevents delegated managers from assigning capabilities or branch reach they do not themselves hold.

after.evidence: Used Oyatie read-only references for pre-execution policy preview/autonomy ceiling and deterministic policy impact discipline. Added backend tests first in backend/crates/identity/rest/src/lib.rs: assignment_ceiling_rejects_capability_actor_does_not_hold, assignment_ceiling_requires_custom_grant_scope_to_cover_role_scope, and assignment_ceiling_requires_elevated_grant_for_user_management_roles. Red evidence: Docker Rust 1.96 cargo test -p mnt-identity-rest policy_role_template_tests::assignment_ceiling_rejects_capability_actor_does_not_hold --lib failed with missing ensure_policy_roles_inside_actor_permission_ceiling. Implemented server-side guard wired into assignment preview and replace paths: requested role branch reach is computed from branch conditions, each non-deny permission must be held by the principal through system roles or runtime effective grants covering that scope, elevated/scope-widening custom features remain rejected, and user_manage assignment requires ElevatedRoleGrant. Verification: Docker Rust 1.96 cargo fmt --all -- --check passed; cargo test -p mnt-identity-rest --lib passed 5/5; cargo clippy -p mnt-identity-rest --all-targets -- -D warnings passed; disposable Postgres org_setup integration passed 20/20; git diff --check passed.
after.rationale: Closes the R2 grant-less-than-or-equal-self assignment choke point before expanding manager-customizable PBAC/ABAC/RBAC; prevents delegated managers from assigning capabilities or branch reach they do not themselves hold.

goals.title: Policy studio — PBAC + ABAC + RBAC
goals.objective: Add a manager/admin-customizable access policy studio that composes role grants (RBAC), attribute conditions (ABAC: group, tenant, org, department, team, position, employment status, assignment, location, device/security posture), and permission/purpose policies (PBAC: action/resource/purpose/sensitive-action/passkey-step-up). The UI must support safe templates, scope preview, diff/impact review, passkey step-up for publishing, audit logging, rollback, and guardrails so managers can only manage policies inside their delegated authority.
goals.evidence: User clarification 2026-06-26: essentially this is PBAC, ABAC, RBAC; these should be customizable within the UI/UX by managers with necessary permissions.

goals.title: Collaboration suite — messenger, mail, calendar, polls
goals.objective: Polish and wire an enterprise collaboration suite: messenger with support escalation/action cards/SLA context; mail inbox/thread workflow beyond settings; calendar with personal work calendar plus shared team/department/org/group calendars, availability, work-order/leave/approval overlays, and conflict visibility; polls/surveys with role-scoped audience targeting, anonymity controls, deadlines, reminders, results, and audit/compliance boundaries. UI must be role-aware, accessible, tested, and safe when backend support is staged.
goals.evidence: User requirement 2026-06-26: polish messenger, mail, calendar, and poll system; calendar has to be both shared(team/org/department etc) + personal(work) calendar.

goals.title: Native mobile collaboration + approvals
goals.objective: Polish the iOS Swift and Android Kotlin employee phone apps around today work/clock-in, passkey signing and sensitive-action authorization, notifications and alerts, approvals such as overtime, messenger, company mail, personal/shared calendar, and polls. Reuse generated APIs and reputable MIT/Apache dependencies only where commodity UI/protocol work should be adopted; own product flows, policy checks, auditability, tests, and native integration.
goals.evidence: User 2026-06-26: not just Swift but Kotlin for Android; phone app primary for clock-in, passkey auth/signing/authorization, notifications/alerts, approvals, messenger, mail, calendar, polls; end product must be well integrated and interact as if native.

goals.evidence: Active aggregate /goal in .omc/ultragoal/plans/conglomerate-platform/goals.json + ledger.jsonl covers G001-G015; marking story G001-track-a-stabilize-live-ops COMPLETE (G002-G015 remain active). Commits 3a87533..0755965. VALIDATION/REVIEW: review-gate (correctness+RLS-as-mnt_rt+codex cross-model+web) = GO-with-fixes, 2 mediums landed; security-reviewer on RBAC sub-spec (separate goal); real mnt_rt tests; backend gates PASSED; full workspace cargo test green after org_setup pagination fix. DEPLOYED LIVE: blue-green /readyz-smoke promotion to mnt-app@804d601+mnt-web@a28c85d, rollouts Healthy, live triager diagnostic done (1/9 admins low-risk). Blocked-external: #19.14 FLMS (Windows MSI). Orthogonal pre-existing CI debt (api-clients/E2E/Android) = task #47.

evidence: Code-reviewer Euler REQUEST CHANGES found preview/replace only checked requested_roles, so a scoped actor could replace with [] and remove an existing out-of-scope or out-of-ceiling custom-role assignment; preview also minted a receipt. Added failing integration regression branch_scoped_policy_managers_cannot_remove_out_of_scope_assignments in backend/crates/identity/rest/tests/org_setup.rs. Red evidence: Docker Rust 1.96 disposable Postgres cargo test -p mnt-identity-rest --test org_setup branch_scoped_policy_managers_cannot_remove_out_of_scope_assignments failed because preview returned 200 with removed_role_ids and preview_receipt_id. Fixed backend/crates/identity/rest/src/lib.rs by loading current assignments in replace, computing roles touched by requested union removed assignments, and applying delegated branch-scope and actor permission ceiling checks to that touched set in both preview and replace before preview receipts or passkey writes. Verification: regression passed; full org_setup integration passed 21/21; cargo fmt --all -- --check passed; cargo test -p mnt-identity-rest --lib passed 5/5; cargo clippy -p mnt-identity-rest --all-targets -- -D warnings passed; git diff --check passed; disposable Postgres was removed.
message: Assignment replacement is a full-set write; removing an existing role is also a policy mutation and must be inside the same delegated authority ceiling as adding it.

after.evidence: Code-reviewer Euler REQUEST CHANGES found preview/replace only checked requested_roles, so a scoped actor could replace with [] and remove an existing out-of-scope or out-of-ceiling custom-role assignment; preview also minted a receipt. Added failing integration regression branch_scoped_policy_managers_cannot_remove_out_of_scope_assignments in backend/crates/identity/rest/tests/org_setup.rs. Red evidence: Docker Rust 1.96 disposable Postgres cargo test -p mnt-identity-rest --test org_setup branch_scoped_policy_managers_cannot_remove_out_of_scope_assignments failed because preview returned 200 with removed_role_ids and preview_receipt_id. Fixed backend/crates/identity/rest/src/lib.rs by loading current assignments in replace, computing roles touched by requested union removed assignments, and applying delegated branch-scope and actor permission ceiling checks to that touched set in both preview and replace before preview receipts or passkey writes. Verification: regression passed; full org_setup integration passed 21/21; cargo fmt --all -- --check passed; cargo test -p mnt-identity-rest --lib passed 5/5; cargo clippy -p mnt-identity-rest --all-targets -- -D warnings passed; git diff --check passed; disposable Postgres was removed.
after.rationale: Assignment replacement is a full-set write; removing an existing role is also a policy mutation and must be inside the same delegated authority ceiling as adding it.

goals.title: Policy studio — PBAC + ABAC + RBAC
goals.objective: Add a manager/admin-customizable access policy studio that composes role grants (RBAC), attribute conditions (ABAC: group, tenant, org, department, team, position, employment status, assignment, location, device/security posture), and permission/purpose policies (PBAC: action/resource/purpose/sensitive-action/passkey-step-up). The UI must support safe templates, scope preview, diff/impact review, passkey step-up for publishing, audit logging, rollback, and guardrails so managers can only manage policies inside their delegated authority.
goals.evidence: User clarification 2026-06-26: essentially this is PBAC, ABAC, RBAC; these should be customizable within the UI/UX by managers with necessary permissions.

goals.evidence: User clarification 2026-06-26: essentially this is PBAC, ABAC, RBAC; these should be customizable within the UI/UX by managers with necessary permissions.

after.evidence: Heisenberg review found TOCTOU: assignment replacement authorized before adapter transaction could delete assignments added after preview. Added red regression assignment_save_rejects_stale_preview_when_current_assignments_changed; it failed before fix because stale receipt deleted a role added after preview. after.rationale: The ledger must preserve the security review finding, red evidence, transactional fix, and verification for G016 Policy Studio.

evidence: Bohr review REQUEST CHANGES found receipt baseline still omitted target branch scope and role-definition/policy-version authorization inputs. Added red regressions assignment_save_rejects_stale_preview_when_target_branches_changed and assignment_save_rejects_stale_preview_when_role_definition_changed; before fix both failed while current-assignment stale test passed. Fixed receipts to store and compare current_branch_ids, current_role_ids, requested role_ids, and policy_version. Receipt creation locks target user and validates branch/current-assignment/policy baselines before minting; replacement locks target user, fetches branch/current-assignment baselines, locks policy version, then consumes only an exact baseline match before delete. Verification: stale-preview filter PASS 3/3; cargo fmt --all -- --check PASS; cargo test -p mnt-identity-rest --lib PASS 5/5; cargo clippy -p mnt-identity-rest --all-targets -- -D warnings PASS; full org_setup PASS 24/24; git diff --check PASS.
message: Closes remaining delegated-scope and role-definition TOCTOU windows identified by independent code review before G016 finalization.

goals.title: Policy studio — PBAC + ABAC + RBAC
goals.objective: Add a manager/admin-customizable access policy studio that composes role grants (RBAC), attribute conditions (ABAC: group, tenant, org, department, team, position, employment status, assignment, location, device/security posture), and permission/purpose policies (PBAC: action/resource/purpose/sensitive-action/passkey-step-up). The UI must support safe templates, scope preview, diff/impact review, passkey step-up for publishing, audit logging, rollback, and guardrails so managers can only manage policies inside their delegated authority.
goals.evidence: User clarification 2026-06-26: essentially this is PBAC, ABAC, RBAC; these should be customizable within the UI/UX by managers with necessary permissions.

goals.title: P3 — Nav fabric
goals.objective: ObjectLink + back-stack breadcrumb + Cmd-K command palette + shared DataTable

goals.title: P4 — Triage Home
goals.objective: /home per-role what-needs-me-now queues (role-workflow backlog as a surface)

goals.title: P5 — Object-Set query API + faceted lens
goals.objective: filter/histogram/listogram/aggregate/search-around (CAP-3) + #24 analytics tiles drill-to-act

goals.title: P6 — Timeline + graph lenses
goals.objective: Equipment lifecycle ribbon + Customer-Site-Equipment-WO graph

goals.title: Action/write-back engine
goals.objective: CAP-5 transactional audited action executor + generated forms (closed-loop)

goals.title: Track C — HR core
goals.objective: Employee/직급/조직도 (#36) + Attendance(clock-in) + Leave

goals.title: Track C — Korean payroll
goals.objective: JIT sub-spec + effective-dated rate tables + golden-case tests + 노무사 validation gate

goals.title: Track C — ERP
goals.objective: 회계 double-entry + 부가세 + 전자세금계산서 relay; 구매매입/AP; 재고; 영업매출/AR (JIT sub-specs)

goals.title: Track D — Webmail enable
goals.objective: #28 Vault key + #39 inbound hardening + webmail client UI (DOMPurify gate) + unify inquiries (#42)

goals.title: Track D — Dispatch map
goals.objective: #29 clock-in-gated live location + arrival/return markers + routing

goals.title: Collaboration suite — messenger, mail, calendar, polls
goals.objective: Polish and wire an enterprise collaboration suite: messenger with support escalation/action cards/SLA context; mail inbox/thread workflow beyond settings; calendar with personal work calendar plus shared team/department/org/group calendars, availability, work-order/leave/approval overlays, and conflict visibility; polls/surveys with role-scoped audience targeting, anonymity controls, deadlines, reminders, results, and audit/compliance boundaries. UI must be role-aware, accessible, tested, and safe when backend support is staged.
goals.evidence: User requirement 2026-06-26: polish messenger, mail, calendar, and poll system; calendar has to be both shared(team/org/department etc) + personal(work) calendar.

goals.title: Native mobile collaboration + approvals
goals.objective: Polish the iOS Swift and Android Kotlin employee phone apps around today work/clock-in, passkey signing and sensitive-action authorization, notifications and alerts, approvals such as overtime, messenger, company mail, personal/shared calendar, and polls. Reuse generated APIs and reputable MIT/Apache dependencies only where commodity UI/protocol work should be adopted; own product flows, policy checks, auditability, tests, and native integration.
goals.evidence: User 2026-06-26: not just Swift but Kotlin for Android; phone app primary for clock-in, passkey auth/signing/authorization, notifications/alerts, approvals, messenger, mail, calendar, polls; end product must be well integrated and interact as if native.

goals.title: Track A — Stabilize live ops
goals.objective: Fix issue-#19 broken-loop flows (#40 batches) + remaining #19; every flow create→admin-visible round-trip tested as real mnt_rt
goals.evidence: Active aggregate /goal in .omc/ultragoal/plans/conglomerate-platform/goals.json + ledger.jsonl covers G001-G015; marking story G001-track-a-stabilize-live-ops COMPLETE (G002-G015 remain active). IMPLEMENTATION: #19 core flows fixed+deployed (#19.13 mgmt_no normalize, #19.17 daily-plan visibility, #19.18 purchase create WORM-defer + error surfacing, #19.21 inquiry org-scope/storefront_org, #19.22 PM-schedule); codex P0 authz (OrgWideQueueTriage replaces is_admin_like widen; daily-plan gate; mgmt_no exact-then-unique binding+preview). Commits 3a87533..0755965. VALIDATION/REVIEW: review-gate (correctness+RLS-as-mnt_rt+codex cross-model+web) = GO-with-fixes, 2 mediums landed; security-reviewer on RBAC sub-spec (separate goal); real mnt_rt tests; backend gates PASSED; full workspace cargo test green after org_setup pagination fix. DEPLOYED LIVE: blue-green /readyz-smoke promotion to mnt-app@804d601+mnt-web@a28c85d, rollouts Healthy, live triager diagnostic done (1/9 admins low-risk). Blocked-external: #19.14 FLMS (Windows MSI). Orthogonal pre-existing CI debt (api-clients/E2E/Android) = task #47.

goals.title: Track B0 — Org-hierarchy security
goals.objective: Write org-hierarchy.md sub-spec + architect + security-review; AccessScope (Group→법인→branch→worksite) generalizing BranchScope + RLS-preserving consolidated reads
goals.evidence: G002 complete: docs/specs/org-hierarchy.md updated; AccessScope enforcement centralizes live DB branch-scope resolution and live-scope intersection in mnt-platform-request-context + mnt-platform-authz; ordinary tenant REST/WS routes now call shared resolver and no production path trusts claims.branches; AccessScope::Group rejected outside RLS-preserving group fan-out; JWT group_roles canonicalized to GROUP_ADMIN/GROUP_VIEWER/GROUP_FINANCE on issue+verify. Verification: git diff --check backend/docs passed; cd backend && cargo fmt --check --all passed; cargo metadata --no-deps passed; static stale-principal scan passed (none missing shared resolver, no production claims.branches); static invariant checks passed; rust-analyzer stdin parse passed for changed Rust files. Independent review: code-reviewer APPROVE no blockers; architect CLEAR. Codex usage for story goal: 925605 tokens, 35m52s.

goals.title: P0 — Blueprint tokens
goals.objective: Semantic tone/color map + Mono/Won + .console scope; re-pin priority/status/SLA color helpers (extends C1-C13)
goals.evidence: G003 complete: added .console-scoped Blueprint semantic tone variables in web/src/styles.css, new shared tone helpers in web/src/lib/semantic.ts, currency/typography primitives in web/src/lib/currency.ts + web/src/lib/format.tsx, and re-pinned work-order priority, dispatch SLA, support status/priority/SLA, and equipment status helpers to shared tone classes. Authenticated AppShell/PlatformShell now carry .console bg-console-canvas while public storefront/PublicLayout remains outside scope. Applied Mono/Won in equipment id rows, equipment detail read rows, and financial asset lifecycle money rows. Tests added/updated: semantic, format, priorityClass, SlaBadge class assertions, support-format tone assertions, equipment-format, AssetLifecycleCostPanel accessible money labels. Verification: npm --workspace web run test -- src/lib/semantic.test.ts src/lib/format.test.tsx src/lib/utils.test.ts src/features/dispatch/SlaBadge.test.tsx src/features/support/support-format.test.ts src/features/equipment/equipment-format.test.ts src/features/financial/AssetLifecycleCostPanel.test.tsx = 7 files/28 tests passed; npm --workspace web run lint passed (eslint + check-ui-strings); npm --workspace web run build passed; git diff --check for touched tracked web files passed; new-file whitespace check passed. Independent review: code-reviewer APPROVE, architect CLEAR. Codex usage for story goal: 288430 tokens, 14m45s.

goals.title: P1 — Object-View kit
goals.objective: Extract ObjectViewScaffold from WorkOrderDetail (no behavior change) + timeline/approval/evidence panels (CAP-2)
goals.evidence: G004 complete: extracted thin domain-neutral ObjectViewScaffold/ObjectViewPanel/ObjectViewProperties/ObjectViewField in web/src/features/object-view; refactored WorkOrderDetail into local identity/narrative/evidence/approval/timeline panels without changing write gates; added read-only approval_line panel with localized role/status labels and tests proving no approver UUID/raw enum leak; preserved enterprise custom-role invariant by changing roleLabel fallback to safeLabel so valid custom roles display while UUIDs remain suppressed. User enterprise-operations and dynamic-role clarifications recorded in Ultragoal ledger. Verification: npm --workspace web run test -- src/features/org/org-format.test.ts src/pages/WorkOrderDetailPage.test.tsx src/features/object-view/ObjectViewScaffold.test.tsx = 3 files/8 tests passed; npm --workspace web run test -- src/features/approvals/ApprovalQueue.test.tsx src/features/dispatch/WorkOrderList.test.tsx src/pages/DispatchPage.test.tsx src/App.test.tsx = 4 files/36 tests passed; npm --workspace web run test -- src/EmptyBackend.test.tsx = 13 tests passed standalone after cross-file MSW interference was isolated; npm --workspace web run lint passed; npm --workspace web run build passed; git diff --check passed. Independent review: code-reviewer APPROVE; architect CLEAR. Codex usage for story goal: 308044 tokens, 16m45s.

goals.title: P2 — Equipment Object View
goals.objective: /equipment/:id via scaffold + links rail + actions; closes imported-equipment-not-viewable (#8)
goals.evidence: G005 complete: added branch-scoped GET /api/v1/equipment/{id} for imported equipment object links, with EquipmentReadQuery, PgRegistryStore::get_equipment under with_org_conn/RLS, REST get_equipment read authorization, OpenAPI getEquipment, and generated TS/Kotlin/Swift clients. Web /equipment/:id now uses the shared ObjectViewScaffold and typed GET-by-id instead of scanning /equipment/list, and browse rows preserve quick-view while the action link navigates to /equipment/{id}. Dynamic/customizable PBAC/ABAC/RBAC roles remain explicitly tracked as G016; this slice did not widen static role architecture beyond existing UI edit affordance gates. Regression: added backend SQLx GET-by-id/404 test (not run because direct cargo test/check is forbidden by repo hook) and web detail/browse tests. Verification: npm --workspace web run test -- src/pages/EquipmentDetailPage.test.tsx src/pages/EquipmentBrowsePage.test.tsx src/features/equipment/EquipmentManagementPanel.test.tsx src/features/equipment/equipment-format.test.ts src/features/object-view/ObjectViewScaffold.test.tsx src/EmptyBackend.test.tsx = 6 files/25 tests passed; npm run check:ts passed; npm --workspace web run lint passed; npm --workspace web run build passed; cd backend && cargo fmt --all && cargo fmt --all -- --check passed; cargo metadata --format-version 1 --no-deps passed with 75 packages/75 workspace members; TS/Kotlin/Swift client regeneration was deterministic/no-op after generation; npm run check:kotlin BUILD SUCCESSFUL; npm run check:swift Build complete; git diff --check passed. Independent review: code-reviewer APPROVE no issues, confirmed no 2000-row list scan remains and branch/RLS boundaries preserved; architect CLEAR, confirmed minimal future-compatible contract and policy-studio gap bounded to G016. Codex story goal complete: 605046 tokens, 26m59s.

after.evidence: Bohr review REQUEST CHANGES found receipt baseline still omitted target branch scope and role-definition/policy-version authorization inputs. Added red regressions assignment_save_rejects_stale_preview_when_target_branches_changed and assignment_save_rejects_stale_preview_when_role_definition_changed; before fix both failed while current-assignment stale test passed. Fixed receipts to store and compare current_branch_ids, current_role_ids, requested role_ids, and policy_version. Receipt creation locks target user and validates branch/current-assignment/policy baselines before minting; replacement locks target user, fetches branch/current-assignment baselines, locks policy version, then consumes only an exact baseline match before delete. Verification: stale-preview filter PASS 3/3; cargo fmt --all -- --check PASS; cargo test -p mnt-identity-rest --lib PASS 5/5; cargo clippy -p mnt-identity-rest --all-targets -- -D warnings PASS; full org_setup PASS 24/24; git diff --check PASS.
after.rationale: Closes remaining delegated-scope and role-definition TOCTOU windows identified by independent code review before G016 finalization.

Receipt creation locks target user and validates branch/current-assignment/policy baselines before minting; replacement locks target user, fetches branch/current-assignment baselines, locks policy version, then consumes only an exact baseline match before delete. Verification: stale-preview filter PASS 3/3; cargo fmt --all -- --check PASS; cargo test -p mnt-identity-rest --lib PASS 5/5; cargo clippy -p mnt-identity-rest --all-targets -- -D warnings PASS; full org_setup PASS 24/24; git diff --check PASS.

goals.title: Policy studio — PBAC + ABAC + RBAC
goals.objective: Add a manager/admin-customizable access policy studio that composes role grants (RBAC), attribute conditions (ABAC: group, tenant, org, department, team, position, employment status, assignment, location, device/security posture), and permission/purpose policies (PBAC: action/resource/purpose/sensitive-action/passkey-step-up). The UI must support safe templates, scope preview, diff/impact review, passkey step-up for publishing, audit logging, rollback, and guardrails so managers can only manage policies inside their delegated authority.
goals.evidence: User clarification 2026-06-26: essentially this is PBAC, ABAC, RBAC; these should be customizable within the UI/UX by managers with necessary permissions.

goals.objective: Polish and wire an enterprise collaboration suite: messenger with support escalation/action cards/SLA context; mail inbox/thread workflow beyond settings; calendar with personal work calendar plus shared team/department/org/group calendars, availability, work-order/leave/approval overlays, and conflict visibility; polls/surveys with role-scoped audience targeting, anonymity controls, deadlines, reminders, results, and audit/compliance boundaries. goals.title: Native mobile collaboration + approvals
goals.objective: Polish the iOS Swift and Android Kotlin employee phone apps around today work/clock-in, passkey signing and sensitive-action authorization, notifications and alerts, approvals such as overtime, messenger, company mail, personal/shared calendar, and polls. Reuse generated APIs and reputable MIT/Apache dependencies only where commodity UI/protocol work should be adopted; own product flows, policy checks, auditability, tests, and native integration.
goals.evidence: User 2026-06-26: not just Swift but Kotlin for Android; phone app primary for clock-in, passkey auth/signing/authorization, notifications/alerts, approvals, messenger, mail, calendar, polls; end product must be well integrated and interact as if native.
